Please note, the base salary range listed below and the benefits in this paragraph are only applicable to U.S.-based candidates.</p></div><div class="title">MongoDB’s base salary range for this role in the U.S. is:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$111,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$218,000 USD</span></div></div></div>

jobs.title: Software Engineer, Networking & Observability
jobs.location: Alberta; British Columbia; Manitoba; Nova Scotia; Ontario; Quebec
jobs.description: <p>Join the MongoDB Networking &amp; Observability team and help build the core of a distributed database ! Our team focuses on creating and enhancing components which facilitate communication between distributed processes and make these processes, and their communication, easily observable. Networking Observability’s responsibilities include improving MongoDB networking, improving the efficiency of resource utilization, and building low-overhead observability features.</p> <p>Our team includes engineers located in New York City and fully remote engineers. We operate close to the bottom of the stack, and have a lot of influence over the availability, performance, and robustness of our open source database as well as the development efficiency of the engineering team overall. Recently we’ve worked on POCs and performance tests for new architectural directions in our networking stack, and are integrating OpenTelemetry to accelerate investigation of issues and integrate with a wider ecosystem of observability tooling.</p> <p>Are you excited to help the MongoDB engineering team build a better database? We are! Join us today, and we can build a faster, more reliable, exceptionally observable, database system together.</p> <p>Note: This role can be based out of our NYC office or remotely from anywhere in North America.</p> <h3>Candidate Profile</h3> <ul> <li>3+ years of experience building distributed systems</li> <li>Passionate about delivering and deploying a product with cross-team stakeholders</li> <li>Solid computer science fundamentals, with strong competencies in data structures, algorithms, and software design/architecture</li> <li>Hands-on experience with building production-level code. Experience in C++ is required</li> <li>Interest in furthering their knowledge of networking, observability and how computer architecture and internals impact the availability</li> <li>Solid verbal and written communication skills and highly motivated to collaborate with colleagues</li> </ul> <h3>Position Expectations</h3> <ul> <li>Understand and improve the current functionality of the MongoDB networking and observability codebase</li> <li>Lead medium sized projects related to our networking code or observability frameworks from technical design to delivery</li> <li>Collaborate with colleagues at all stages of project lifecycle (ideation, requirements gathering, design, execution, and delivery)</li> <li>Own our <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company/leadership-principles">leadership principles</a>, and exemplify them in your work</li> <li>Continue to learn and be curious in growing your career</li> </ul> <h3>Success Measures</h3> <ul> <li>In one month, you'll have fixed a few bugs in a minor version of our latest release series</li> <li>In three months you’ll have contributed to the development of a project slated for the next major version</li> <li>In six months, you’ll have taken on primary code review responsibilities and are involved in reviewing the design for new features</li> <li>In twelve months, you’re making significant contributions to the development of a new major features</li> </ul> <p style="margin: 0px !important; padding: 0px !important;"><span style="color: #000000; background-color: transparent;">About MongoDB</span></p> <p style="margin: 0px !important; padding: 0px !important;">&nbsp;</p> <p style="margin: 0px !important; padding: 0px !important;"><span style="color: #000000; background-color: transparent;">MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the database for the AI era, enabling innovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. MongoDB’s unified database platform—the most widely available, globally distributed database on the market—helps organizations modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our cloud-native platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi-cloud database and is available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.</span></p> <p style="margin: 0px !important; padding: 0px !important;">&nbsp;</p> <p style="margin: 0px !important; padding: 0px !important;"><span style="color: #000000; background-color: transparent;">With offices worldwide and nearly 60,000 customers—including 75% of the Fortune 100 and AI-native startups—relying on MongoDB for their most important applications, we’re powering the next era of software.</span></p> <p style="margin: 0px !important; padding: 0px !important;">&nbsp;</p> <p style="margin: 0px !important; padding: 0px !important;"><span style="color: #000000; background-color: transparent;">Our compass at MongoDB is our </span><u style="color: #1155cc; background-color: transparent;"><a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company">Leadership Commitment,</a></u><span style="color: #000000; background-color: transparent;"> guiding how and why we make decisions, show up for each other, and win. It’s what makes us MongoDB.&nbsp;</span></p> <p style="margin: 0px !important; padding: 0px !important;">&nbsp;</p> <p style="margin: 0px !important; padding: 0px !important;"><span style="color: #000000; background-color: transparent;">To drive the personal growth and business impact of our employees, we’re committed to developing a supportive and enriching culture for everyone. </span><u style="color: #1155cc; background-color: transparent;"><a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company/blog/culture/employee-benefits-that-make-a-difference-at-mongodb">From employee affinity groups, to fertility assistance and a generous parental leave policy</a></u><span style="color: #000000; background-color: transparent;">, we value our employees’ wellbeing and want to support them along every step of their professional and personal journeys.</span><a style="color: #000000; background-color: transparent;" href="https://www.mongodb.com/blog/channel/culture"> </a><u style="color: #1155cc; background-color: transparent;"><a href="https://www.mongodb.com/blog/channel/culture">Learn more about what it’s like to work at MongoDB</a></u><span style="color: #000000; background-color: transparent;">, and help us make an impact on the world!</span></p> <p style="margin: 0px !important; padding: 0px !important;">&nbsp;</p> <p style="margin: 0px !important; padding: 0px !important;"><span style="color: #000000; background-color: transparent;">MongoDB is committed to providing any necessary accommodations for individuals with disabilities within our application and interview process. To request an accommodation due to a disability, please inform your recruiter.</span></p> <p style="margin: 0px !important; padding: 0px !important;">&nbsp;</p> <p style="margin: 0px !important; padding: 0px !important;"><span style="color: #000000; background-color: transparent;">MongoDB is an equal opportunities employer.</span></p> <p style="margin: 0px !important; padding: 0px !important;">&nbsp;</p> <p style="margin: 0px !important; padding: 0px !important;"><span style="color: #000000; background-color: transparent;">Req ID: 1273376223</span></p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p>MongoDB’s base salary range for this role is posted below. Please note, the base salary range listed below and the benefits in this paragraph are only applicable to candidates based in Canada.</p></div><div class="title">MongoDB’s base salary range for this role in Canada is:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$108,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$149,000 CAD</span></div></div><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><em>AI is used to review applications based on job-related criteria and does not replace human decision-making. jobs.title: Content Designer
jobs.description: <h2><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><strong>About the role</strong></span></h2> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Chime is building a better way to bank—one that’s driven by purpose, not profit. We’re looking for a Content Designer to join our Product Design team and help shape consumer-facing product experiences used by millions of members.</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">As part of a small, highly impactful content design function, you’ll have ownership over content within the Spending, Savings &amp; Investing pillar. You’ll work with designers, product managers, researchers, engineers, and product marketers to bring clarity to complex financial concepts and ensure content meaningfully improves the member experience.</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">The base salary offered for this role and level of experience will begin at $170,000 and up to $235,000. Full-time employees are also eligible for a bonus, competitive equity package, and benefits. The actual base salary offered may be higher, depending on your location, skills, qualifications, and experience.&nbsp;</span></p> <h2><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><strong>In this role, you can expect to</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Own content design for defined product areas, driving clarity and quality across end-to-end user experiences in Chime’s mobile apps and digital product surfaces.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Partner closely with cross-functional teams to shape product direction through content, from early concepting through launch and iteration.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Write and refine clear, inclusive, and human content for product flows, feature messaging, system feedback, and microcopy.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Make informed content decisions within your problem spaces, contributing to naming, taxonomy, and information hierarchy.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Apply and evolve Chime’s product voice and tone in collaboration with Design and Brand partners.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Use research insights, data, and experimentation to validate content decisions and continuously improve outcomes.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Help scale content design at Chime by contributing to guidelines, documentation, and lightweight systems that enable consistency and quality.</span></li> </ul> <h2><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><strong>To thrive in this role, you have</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Experience designing content for consumer-facing digital products, with a strong understanding of UX principles and mobile-first best practices.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">A portfolio that demonstrates end-to-end ownership, user-centered writing, and the ability to operate with a high degree of autonomy.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Comfort leading content decisions in ambiguous spaces and influencing cross-functional partners through craft and rationale.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Ability to think holistically about user journeys while maintaining a high bar for in-product copy.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Experience contributing to content systems, guidelines, or scalable patterns in a product environment.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Strong communication skills, particularly when explaining content decisions and tradeoffs to partners with different perspectives.</span></li> </ul> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">#LI-TP1 #LI-Onsite</span></p><div class="content-conclusion"><h2><strong>A little about us</strong></h2> <p>At Chime, we believe that everyone can achieve financial progress. We created Chime—a financial technology company, not a bank*—on the premise that core banking services should be helpful, easy, and free. Through our user-friendly tools and intuitive platforms, we empower our members to take control of their finances and work towards their goals. Whether it's starting a savings account, purchasing a first car or home, launching a business, or pursuing higher education, we're proud to have helped millions unlock their financial potential.</p> <p>We're a team of problem solvers, dreamers, and builders with one shared obsession: our members. From day one, Chimers have worked tirelessly to out-hustle and out-execute competitors to bring our mission to life. Their grit and determination inspire us to work harder every day to deliver the very best experience possible. We each bring an owner's mindset to our work, refusing to be outdone and holding ourselves accountable to meet and exceed the highest bars for our teams, our company, and our members.</p> <p>We believe in being bold, dreaming big, and taking risks, while also working together, embracing our diverse perspectives, and giving each other honest feedback. Our culture remains deeply entrepreneurial, encouraging every Chimer to see themselves as stewards of our mission to help everyday Americans unlock their financial progress.&nbsp;</p> <p>We know that to achieve our mission, we must earn and keep people's trust—so we hold ourselves to the highest standards of integrity in everything we do. These aren't just words on a wall—our values are embedded in every aspect of our business, serving as a north star that guides us as we work to help millions achieve their financial potential.</p> <p>Because if we don't—who will?</p> <p>*Chime is a financial technology company, not a bank. Banking services provided by The Bancorp Bank, N.A. or Stride Bank, N.A., Members FDIC.</p> <h2><strong>What we offer for our full-time, regular employees</strong></h2> <ul> <li>🏢 Our in-office work policy is designed to keep you connected - with four days a week in the office and Fridays from home for those near one of our offices, plus team and company-wide events depending on location. Whether you’re coming in regularly or are part of our fully remote program, you’ll stay engaged with your work and teammates.</li> <li>💻 In-office perks including backup child, elder, and/or pet care, plus a subsidized commuter benefit to support your regular commute</li> <li>💰 Competitive salary based on experience</li> <li>✨ 401k match plus great medical, dental, vision, life, and disability benefits</li> <li>🏝 Generous vacation policy and company-wide Chime Days, bonus company-wide paid days off</li> <li>🫂 1% of your time off to support local community organizations of your choice</li> <li>👟 Annual wellness stipend to use towards eligible wellness related expenses</li> <li>👶 Up to 24 weeks of paid parental leave for birthing parents and 12 weeks of paid parental leave for non-birthing parents</li> <li>👪 Access to Maven, a family planning tool, with $15k lifetime reimbursement for egg freezing, fertility treatments, adoption, and more.</li> <li>🎉 In-person and virtual events to connect with your fellow Chimers—think cooking classes, guided meditations, music festivals, mixology classes, paint nights, etc., and delicious snack boxes, too!</li> <li>💚 A challenging and fulfilling opportunity to join one of the most experienced teams in FinTech and help millions unlock financial progress</li> </ul> <p>We know that great work can’t be done without a diverse team and inclusive environment. That’s why we specifically look for individuals of varying strengths, skills, backgrounds, and ideas to join our team. We believe this gives us a competitive advantage to better serve our members and helps us all grow as Chimers and individuals.</p> <p>Chime is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We consider qualified applicants without regard to race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, marital or family status, disability, genetic information, veteran status, or any other legally protected basis under provincial, federal, state, and local laws, regulations, or ordinances. We will also consider qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with the requirements of state and local laws, including the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, Cook County Ordinance, NYC Fair Chance Act, and the LA City Fair Chance Ordinance, and consistent with Canadian provincial and federal laws. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation during any stage of the application process, please contact: <a href="mailto:benefits@chime.com">benefits@chime.com</a>.</p> <p>To learn more about how Chime collects and uses your personal information during the application process, please see the&nbsp;<a href="https://www.chime.com/careers/chime-applicant-privacy-notice/">Chime Applicant Privacy Notice. </a></p></div>

jobs.title: Mobile Full-Stack Engineer, Multiplayer
jobs.description: <h2><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><strong>About the role&nbsp;</strong></span></h2> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">We’re looking for a Full-Stack Engineer to help build some of Chime’s newest and most impactful member experiences within our Spending, Savings &amp; Investing organization. This role will contribute to <strong>Multiplayer projects such as Joint &amp; Teen accounts</strong> - enabling shared financial experiences for families, as well as new initiatives exploring <strong>next-generation banking infrastructure </strong>that can improve speed, flexibility, and access to financial products.</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">These efforts introduce new financial product constructs and require evolving core account systems while delivering seamless member experiences. You’ll work closely with engineers, product managers, and designers to ship scalable, reliable features that support millions of Chime members.</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">The base salary offered for this role and level of experience will begin at $133,000 and up to $184,000. Full-time employees are also eligible for a bonus, competitive equity package, and benefits. The actual base salary offered may be higher, depending on your location, skills, qualifications, and experience.</span></p> <h2><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><strong>In this role, you can expect to</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Independently own and deliver feature-level work end-to-end, from implementation through testing and deployment</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Build product features across backend services and client applications</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Collaborate with engineers, product managers, and designers to deliver member-facing capabilities</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Write clean, reliable, and maintainable code across services and client applications</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Participate in code reviews and contribute to design discussions and implementation planning</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Contribute to improving performance, reliability, and scalability of systems</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Work across teams to support the evolution of core systems and platform capabilities</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Engineers are required to participate in on-call rotation. Being on call may include responding to incidents outside of regular working hours when necessary.</span></li> </ul> <h2><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><strong>To thrive in this role, you have</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">3+ years of experience building production-grade software, with experience shipping user-facing features</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Experience contributing to features end-to-end, including implementation, testing, and deployment</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Experience building backend services and APIs, with an understanding of service-based architectures and working across client applications, including web (e.g., React) and mobile (e.g., React Native), to build user-facing features</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Familiarity with scalable systems and best practices for performance and reliability</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Ability to work with some ambiguity and navigate problems within established systems and patterns</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Strong collaboration skills, with experience working with engineers, product managers, and designers</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">A growth mindset with a focus on ownership, learning, and continuous improvement</span></li> </ul> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">#LI-Hybrid #LI-AA1</span></p><div class="content-conclusion"><h2><strong>A little about us</strong></h2> <p>At Chime, we believe that everyone can achieve financial progress. We created Chime—a financial technology company, not a bank*—on the premise that core banking services should be helpful, easy, and free. Through our user-friendly tools and intuitive platforms, we empower our members to take control of their finances and work towards their goals. Whether it's starting a savings account, purchasing a first car or home, launching a business, or pursuing higher education, we're proud to have helped millions unlock their financial potential.</p> <p>We're a team of problem solvers, dreamers, and builders with one shared obsession: our members. From day one, Chimers have worked tirelessly to out-hustle and out-execute competitors to bring our mission to life. Their grit and determination inspire us to work harder every day to deliver the very best experience possible. We each bring an owner's mindset to our work, refusing to be outdone and holding ourselves accountable to meet and exceed the highest bars for our teams, our company, and our members.</p> <p>We believe in being bold, dreaming big, and taking risks, while also working together, embracing our diverse perspectives, and giving each other honest feedback. Our culture remains deeply entrepreneurial, encouraging every Chimer to see themselves as stewards of our mission to help everyday Americans unlock their financial progress.&nbsp;</p> <p>We know that to achieve our mission, we must earn and keep people's trust—so we hold ourselves to the highest standards of integrity in everything we do. These aren't just words on a wall—our values are embedded in every aspect of our business, serving as a north star that guides us as we work to help millions achieve their financial potential.</p> <p>Because if we don't—who will?</p> <p>*Chime is a financial technology company, not a bank. Banking services provided by The Bancorp Bank, N.A. or Stride Bank, N.A., Members FDIC.</p> <h2><strong>What we offer for our full-time, regular employees</strong></h2> <ul> <li>🏢 Our in-office work policy is designed to keep you connected - with four days a week in the office and Fridays from home for those near one of our offices, plus team and company-wide events depending on location. Whether you’re coming in regularly or are part of our fully remote program, you’ll stay engaged with your work and teammates.</li> <li>💻 In-office perks including backup child, elder, and/or pet care, plus a subsidized commuter benefit to support your regular commute</li> <li>💰 Competitive salary based on experience</li> <li>✨ 401k match plus great medical, dental, vision, life, and disability benefits</li> <li>🏝 Generous vacation policy and company-wide Chime Days, bonus company-wide paid days off</li> <li>🫂 1% of your time off to support local community organizations of your choice</li> <li>👟 Annual wellness stipend to use towards eligible wellness related expenses</li> <li>👶 Up to 24 weeks of paid parental leave for birthing parents and 12 weeks of paid parental leave for non-birthing parents</li> <li>👪 Access to Maven, a family planning tool, with $15k lifetime reimbursement for egg freezing, fertility treatments, adoption, and more.</li> <li>🎉 In-person and virtual events to connect with your fellow Chimers—think cooking classes, guided meditations, music festivals, mixology classes, paint nights, etc., and delicious snack boxes, too!</li> <li>💚 A challenging and fulfilling opportunity to join one of the most experienced teams in FinTech and help millions unlock financial progress</li> </ul> <p>We know that great work can’t be done without a diverse team and inclusive environment. That’s why we specifically look for individuals of varying strengths, skills, backgrounds, and ideas to join our team. We believe this gives us a competitive advantage to better serve our members and helps us all grow as Chimers and individuals.</p> <p>Chime is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We consider qualified applicants without regard to race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, marital or family status, disability, genetic information, veteran status, or any other legally protected basis under provincial, federal, state, and local laws, regulations, or ordinances. We will also consider qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with the requirements of state and local laws, including the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, Cook County Ordinance, NYC Fair Chance Act, and the LA City Fair Chance Ordinance, and consistent with Canadian provincial and federal laws. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation during any stage of the application process, please contact: <a href="mailto:benefits@chime.com">benefits@chime.com</a>.</p> <p>To learn more about how Chime collects and uses your personal information during the application process, please see the&nbsp;<a href="https://www.chime.com/careers/chime-applicant-privacy-notice/">Chime Applicant Privacy Notice. </a></p></div>

jobs.title: Area Manager
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><h2><strong>About Flexport:&nbsp;</strong></h2> <p>At Flexport, we believe global trade can move the human race forward. That’s why it’s our mission to make global commerce so easy there will be more of it. We’re shaping the future of a $10T industry with solutions powered by innovative technology and exceptional people. Today, companies of all sizes—from emerging brands to Fortune 500s—use Flexport technology to move more than $19B of merchandise across 112 countries a year.&nbsp;</p> <p>The recent global supply chain crisis has put Flexport center stage as we continue to play a pivotal role in how goods move around the world. We are proud to have the support of the best investors in the game who believe in our mission, solutions and people. Ready to tackle global challenges that impact business, society, and the environment? Come join us.</p></div><h2>The Opportunity:</h2> <p>We are currently seeking individuals for a full-time position that involves launching and operating one of our inaugural fulfillment centers. As a key member of the Flexport team, you will take on the role of a people leader, responsible for managing a process and a team on the operating floor. This position offers a unique opportunity to invest in others and cultivate a collaborative leadership style. By utilizing the tools, processes, and operations that drive Flexport's mission to make global trade easy for everyone, you will contribute to shaping the future of logistics. We are looking for high-potential candidates who are eager to take on this challenge and be part of our dynamic and innovative organization. Join us in our mission to revolutionize global trade and make a lasting impact on the industry.</p> <p>This role is onsite in Atlanta, GA.&nbsp;</p> <h2>You will:</h2> <ul> <li>Coach, manage, and develop a team of 30-100.</li> <li>Communicate policies to your team and act as the primary information source for the team, maintaining compliance, consistency, and taking corrective action when needed.</li> <li>Create, manage, and support recognition programs across your department.</li> <li>Support all safety programs and OSHA compliance to ensure a safe work environment for all associates.</li> <li>Ensure procedures are followed for building security and product loss prevention.</li> <li>Be an integral part of our Operations team as an Area Manager of one of our largest fulfillment partners located across the country.</li> <li>Own performance reporting for your areas of responsibility within the FC, including, but not limited to, on-time receiving and shipping, inventory accuracy, and packing quality.</li> <li>Consistently hold yourself and your team to strict SLA expectations, providing clear and concise verbal and written reports of performance and action plans when needed.</li> <li>Facilitate troubleshooting with relevant cross-functional parties for fulfillment-related problems.</li> <li>Serve as an escalation point to mitigate risks and resolve issues.</li> <li>Measure and respond to gaps in inventory accuracy, facilitating strategic initiatives to identify root causes and resolve them.</li> <li>Review defect rates to identify trends and expose opportunities to improve operations both internally and across the network.</li> <li>Track learnings and best practices to contribute to Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).</li> <li>Help prioritize the resolution of systemic problems, working closely with our Growth and Engineering teams.</li> </ul> <h2>You should have:</h2> <ul> <li>2+ years managing staff of 5 or more.</li> <li>Must be able to lift up to 49 pounds with or without reasonable accommodation.</li> <li>Must be willing and able to frequently push, pull, squat, bend, and reach.</li> <li>Must be able to stand/walk for up to 10-12 hours.</li> <li>Must be able to work in an environment where the noise level varies and can be loud.</li> <li>Must be able to work in an environment where the temperature may vary between 60 and 90 degrees and will occasionally exceed 90 degrees.</li> <li>Must be able to work on a secure mezzanine at a height of up to 40 feet (applies to buildings with mezzanines). This position requires a candidate with the proven ability to drive process improvements and the ability to keep pace with our growth while motivating others to meet challenges in a deadline-driven environment.</li> <li>Willing and able to work any permanent shift that may include overnights and holidays. Note that there is a high chance that your shift may overlap into part of the weekend.</li> <li>Demonstrated collaboration with internal and external business partners to deliver greater operational performance, efficiency, and quality.</li> <li>Precise communication around external partner decisions, issues, risks, and new processes.</li> <li>Keen ability to distinguish internal versus external problems causes to properly focus the correct resources to resolve issues quickly.</li> <li>Exceptional prioritization skills to ensure timely resolutions that are appropriate for the severity and impact of the situation.</li> <li>Experience leveraging problem-solving and data analysis skills to drive high judgment decisions.</li> <li>Strong presentation and interpersonal skills.</li> <li>Demonstrated ability to work in a fast-paced, ambiguous, deadline-oriented work environment.</li> <li>Ability to adapt in a rapid growth, startup environment.</li> <li>Strong problem-solving skills.</li> <li>Great attention to detail + appreciation for the complexity associated with the consistent achievement of operational tasks.</li> <li>Personable and collaborative; strong affinity for working cross-functionally.</li> <li>Strong computer literacy skills, including usage of Google Sheets/Excel.</li> <li>Incredibly hardworking and willing to do what it takes for us to reach our goals.</li> <li>Experience in warehousing, fulfillment, or transportation/logistics management.</li> <li>Experience working with SQL, creating and running reports.</li> </ul> <p>#LI-Onsite</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><div class="c-message_kit__gutter"> <div class="c-message_kit__gutter__right" data-qa="message_content"> <div class="c-message_kit__blocks c-message_kit__blocks--rich_text"> <div class="c-message__message_blocks c-message__message_blocks--rich_text" data-qa="message-text"> <div class="p-block_kit_renderer" data-qa="block-kit-renderer"> <div class="p-block_kit_renderer__block_wrapper p-block_kit_renderer__block_wrapper--first"> <div class="p-rich_text_block"> <div class="p-rich_text_section"> <div><span style="font-family: arial, sans-serif;"><em>The range displayed on each job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire salaries for the position in the posting's respective region. jobs.title: Area Manager- New Grad
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><h2><strong>About Flexport:&nbsp;</strong></h2> <p>At Flexport, we believe global trade can move the human race forward. That’s why it’s our mission to make global commerce so easy there will be more of it. We’re shaping the future of a $10T industry with solutions powered by innovative technology and exceptional people. Today, companies of all sizes—from emerging brands to Fortune 500s—use Flexport technology to move more than $19B of merchandise across 112 countries a year.&nbsp;</p> <p>The recent global supply chain crisis has put Flexport center stage as we continue to play a pivotal role in how goods move around the world. We are proud to have the support of the best investors in the game who believe in our mission, solutions and people. Ready to tackle global challenges that impact business, society, and the environment? Come join us.</p></div><h2>The Opportunity:</h2> <p>We are currently seeking individuals for a full-time position that involves launching and operating one of our inaugural fulfillment centers. As a key member of the Flexport team, you will take on the role of a people leader, responsible for managing a process and a team on the operating floor. This position offers a unique opportunity to invest in others and cultivate a collaborative leadership style. By utilizing the tools, processes, and operations that drive Flexport's mission to make global trade easy for everyone, you will contribute to shaping the future of logistics. We are looking for high-potential candidates who are eager to take on this challenge and be part of our dynamic and innovative organization. Join us in our mission to revolutionize global trade and make a lasting impact on the industry.</p> <p>This role is onsite in Atlanta, GA.&nbsp;</p> <h2>You will:</h2> <ul> <li>Coach, manage, and develop a team of 30-100.</li> <li>Communicate policies to your team and act as the primary information source for the team, maintaining compliance, consistency, and taking corrective action when needed.</li> <li>Create, manage, and support recognition programs across your department.</li> <li>Support all safety programs and OSHA compliance to ensure a safe work environment for all associates.</li> <li>Ensure procedures are followed for building security and product loss prevention.</li> <li>Be an integral part of our Operations team as an Area Manager of one of our largest fulfillment partners located across the country.</li> <li>Own performance reporting for your areas of responsibility within the FC, including, but not limited to, on-time receiving and shipping, inventory accuracy, and packing quality.</li> <li>Consistently hold yourself and your team to strict SLA expectations, providing clear and concise verbal and written reports of performance and action plans when needed.</li> <li>Facilitate troubleshooting with relevant cross-functional parties for fulfillment-related problems.</li> <li>Serve as an escalation point to mitigate risks and resolve issues.</li> <li>Measure and respond to gaps in inventory accuracy, facilitating strategic initiatives to identify root causes and resolve them.</li> <li>Review defect rates to identify trends and expose opportunities to improve operations both internally and across the network.</li> <li>Track learnings and best practices to contribute to Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).</li> <li>Help prioritize the resolution of systemic problems, working closely with our Growth and Engineering teams.</li> </ul> <h2>You should have:</h2> <ul> <li>2+ years managing staff of 5 or more.</li> <li>Must be able to lift up to 49 pounds with or without reasonable accommodation.</li> <li>Must be willing and able to frequently push, pull, squat, bend, and reach.</li> <li>Must be able to stand/walk for up to 10-12 hours.</li> <li>Must be able to work in an environment where the noise level varies and can be loud.</li> <li>Must be able to work in an environment where the temperature may vary between 60 and 90 degrees and will occasionally exceed 90 degrees.</li> <li>Must be able to work on a secure mezzanine at a height of up to 40 feet (applies to buildings with mezzanines). This position requires a candidate with the proven ability to drive process improvements and the ability to keep pace with our growth while motivating others to meet challenges in a deadline-driven environment.</li> <li>Willing and able to work any permanent shift that may include overnights and holidays. Note that there is a high chance that your shift may overlap into part of the weekend.</li> <li>Demonstrated collaboration with internal and external business partners to deliver greater operational performance, efficiency, and quality.</li> <li>Precise communication around external partner decisions, issues, risks, and new processes.</li> <li>Keen ability to distinguish internal versus external problems causes to properly focus the correct resources to resolve issues quickly.</li> <li>Exceptional prioritization skills to ensure timely resolutions that are appropriate for the severity and impact of the situation.</li> <li>Experience leveraging problem-solving and data analysis skills to drive high judgment decisions.</li> <li>Strong presentation and interpersonal skills.</li> <li>Demonstrated ability to work in a fast-paced, ambiguous, deadline-oriented work environment.</li> <li>Ability to adapt in a rapid growth, startup environment.</li> <li>Strong problem-solving skills.</li> <li>Great attention to detail + appreciation for the complexity associated with the consistent achievement of operational tasks.</li> <li>Personable and collaborative; strong affinity for working cross-functionally.</li> <li>Strong computer literacy skills, including usage of Google Sheets/Excel.</li> <li>Incredibly hardworking and willing to do what it takes for us to reach our goals.</li> <li>Experience in warehousing, fulfillment, or transportation/logistics management.</li> <li>Experience working with SQL, creating and running reports.</li> </ul> <p>#LI-Onsite</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><div class="c-message_kit__gutter"> <div class="c-message_kit__gutter__right" data-qa="message_content"> <div class="c-message_kit__blocks c-message_kit__blocks--rich_text"> <div class="c-message__message_blocks c-message__message_blocks--rich_text" data-qa="message-text"> <div class="p-block_kit_renderer" data-qa="block-kit-renderer"> <div class="p-block_kit_renderer__block_wrapper p-block_kit_renderer__block_wrapper--first"> <div class="p-rich_text_block"> <div class="p-rich_text_section"> <div><span style="font-family: arial, sans-serif;"><em>The range displayed on each job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire salaries for the position in the posting's respective region. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within the range displayed, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education and / or training.&nbsp;</em></span></div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div></div><div class="title">The US base salary range for this position (this does not include bonus, equity and benefits):</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$64,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$81,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><div id="1704749847.557549" class="c-virtual_list__item" data-qa="virtual-list-item" data-item-key="1704749847.557549"> <div class="c-message_kit__background p-message_pane_message__message c-message_kit__message" data-qa="message_container" data-qa-unprocessed="false" data-qa-placeholder="false"> <div class="c-message_kit__hover" data-qa-hover="true"> <div class="c-message_kit__actions c-message_kit__actions--default"> <div class="c-message_kit__gutter"> <div class="c-message_kit__gutter__right" data-qa="message_content"> <div class="c-message_kit__blocks c-message_kit__blocks--rich_text"> <div class="c-message__message_blocks c-message__message_blocks--rich_text" data-qa="message-text"> <div class="p-block_kit_renderer" data-qa="block-kit-renderer"> <div class="p-block_kit_renderer__block_wrapper p-block_kit_renderer__block_wrapper--first"> <div class="p-rich_text_block"> <h4>Commitment to Equal Opportunity</h4> <p>At Flexport, our ability to fulfill our mission of making global commerce easy and accessible relies on having a diverse, dedicated and engaged workforce. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment regardless of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, physical and mental disability, health status, marital and family status, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, military and veteran status, and any other characteristic protected by applicable law.</p> <h4>Global Data Privacy Notice for Job Candidates and Applicants</h4> <p>Depending on your location, the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) may regulate the way we manage the data of job applicants.&nbsp;By submitting your application, you are agreeing to our use and processing of your data as required. Please see our Privacy Notice available at&nbsp;<a href="http://www.flexport.com/privacy" target="_blank" data-saferedirecturl="https://www.google.com/url?q=http://www.flexport.com/privacy&amp;source=gmail&amp;ust=1613840050053000&amp;usg=AFQjCNHD63svlkD-hWHlGuGOM_bSYVrnpw">www.flexport.com/privacy</a>&nbsp;for additional information.</p> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div></div>

jobs.title: Software Engineer II
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><h2><strong>About Flexport:&nbsp;</strong></h2> <p>At Flexport, we believe global trade can move the human race forward. That’s why it’s our mission to make global commerce so easy there will be more of it. We’re shaping the future of a $10T industry with solutions powered by innovative technology and exceptional people. Today, companies of all sizes—from emerging brands to Fortune 500s—use Flexport technology to move more than $19B of merchandise across 112 countries a year.&nbsp;</p> <p>The recent global supply chain crisis has put Flexport center stage as we continue to play a pivotal role in how goods move around the world. We are proud to have the support of the best investors in the game who believe in our mission, solutions and people. Ready to tackle global challenges that impact business, society, and the environment? Come join us.</p></div><h3><strong>The Opportunity</strong></h3> <p>Join a new engineering team in San Francisco building Flexport’s client-facing rates platform and self-serve freight booking experience from the ground up. You will work alongside experienced engineers and have direct ownership of features that move the company from an assisted-sales model to a tech-run one for the long tail of our client base.</p> <p>This team owns two of the highest-leverage surfaces in the Client App: how clients see pricing and how they book without manual intervention. Rates sit at the center of every freight decision, and you will build the systems that power rate visibility, quoting, and pricing intelligence across ocean, air, and trucking. You will also help build the infrastructure that integrates AI-powered automation into the booking flow. This is a rare opportunity to build the "front door" of an $8.6 trillion industry, turning the complexity of global trade into a seamless digital experience for thousands of shippers.</p> <h3><strong>You Will</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Design, develop, test, deploy, and maintain systems within a microservices architecture that power rate visibility and autonomous booking.</li> <li>Own the entire lifecycle of a feature: from database schema design and scalable backend APIs to building the React-based frontend applications our customers use.</li> <li>Support the integration of AI models and automated workflows to make the pricing and booking experience increasingly intelligent.</li> <li>Collaborate with engineering, product, and design to translate complex business problems into elegant, production-ready technical solutions.</li> <li>Contribute to code reviews to maintain high quality standards, share knowledge, and mentor peer engineers.</li> <li>Build highly available, secure, and distributed systems that can scale with Flexport’s global volume.</li> <li>Rapidly build and launch products incrementally, leveraging best practices around CI/CD and shipping to production dozens of times per day.</li> <li>Ensure high-quality standards by incorporating metrics, monitoring, and applying learnings from post-mortems to all services.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>You Should Have</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.</li> <li>3+ years of professional software engineering experience with a proven track record of shipping production-grade applications.</li> <li><strong>Full-Stack Capability:</strong> You are comfortable owning a feature from end-to-end: working with databases, backend services (Java/Ruby), and frontend frameworks (React).</li> <li><strong>Technical Rigor:</strong> A passion for simple, well-tested code that is easy to extend and hard to break.</li> <li><strong>AI Familiarity:</strong> Experience or interest in working with AI APIs and building software that leverages machine learning models to automate user tasks.</li> <li>2+ years of design and architecture knowledge as well as familiarity with object-oriented analysis and design patterns.</li> <li>Proficiency with modern programming languages: specifically Java, Ruby, or TypeScript.</li> <li>Proficiency with deploying, monitoring, and maintaining production services on cloud infrastructure (AWS).</li> </ul> <h3><strong>It’s a Plus If You Have</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Experience with elements of our tech stack: Java, Spring Boot, Ruby on Rails, React, AWS.</li> <li>Experience in fintech, pricing engines, or booking systems.</li> <li>Experience in logistics, supply chain, or freight technology.</li> <li><strong>A bias for action:</strong> When you see a problem, you fix it.</li> <li>Excellent written and verbal communication skills: you can convey technical ideas in common terms to business stakeholders and product managers.</li> </ul> <p>#LI-onsite</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><div class="c-message_kit__gutter"> <div class="c-message_kit__gutter__right" data-qa="message_content"> <div class="c-message_kit__blocks c-message_kit__blocks--rich_text"> <div Within the range displayed, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education and / or training.&nbsp;</em></span></div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div></div><div class="title">The US base salary range for this position (this does not include bonus, equity and benefits):</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$147,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$183,750 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><div id="1704749847.557549" class="c-virtual_list__item" data-qa="virtual-list-item" data-item-key="1704749847.557549"> <div class="c-message_kit__background p-message_pane_message__message c-message_kit__message" data-qa="message_container" data-qa-unprocessed="false" data-qa-placeholder="false"> <div class="c-message_kit__hover" data-qa-hover="true"> <div class="c-message_kit__actions c-message_kit__actions--default"> <div class="c-message_kit__gutter"> <div class="c-message_kit__gutter__right" data-qa="message_content"> <div class="c-message_kit__blocks c-message_kit__blocks--rich_text"> <div class="c-message__message_blocks c-message__message_blocks--rich_text" data-qa="message-text"> <div class="p-block_kit_renderer" data-qa="block-kit-renderer"> <div class="p-block_kit_renderer__block_wrapper p-block_kit_renderer__block_wrapper--first"> <div class="p-rich_text_block"> <h4>Commitment to Equal Opportunity</h4> <p>At Flexport, our ability to fulfill our mission of making global commerce easy and accessible relies on having a diverse, dedicated and engaged workforce. jobs.title: Product Growth Partner
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p>From Fivetran’s founding until now, our mission has remained the same: to make access to data as simple and reliable as electricity. With Fivetran, customer data arrives in their warehouses, canonical and ready to query, with no engineering or maintenance required. We’re proud that more organizations continue to leverage our technology every day to become truly data-driven.</p></div><p><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong><strong>About the Role</strong></strong></span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Fivetran is the one platform for moving the world’s data from every source to every destination to power every innovation.</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">To support our growth, our Product Growth Partners (PGPs) are central to Fivetran’s self-service motion, enabling thousands of customers to succeed at scale. PGPs design and deliver many-to-1 enablement programs, proactively reduce friction for self-service users, and intervene with precision when accounts need additional guidance. PGPs ensure that customers who can self-serve do so seamlessly, while those who need help receive the right support at the right time.</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">This is a full-time, hybrid position based out of our Denver, CO office. Our hybrid work model offers a blend of remote flexibility and in-person collaboration, including two days in the office each week to connect and build as a team.</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>What You'll Do</strong></span></p> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong><strong>Accelerate Self-Service Success</strong></strong></span> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Guide new trial users and existing customers to value through scalable programs such as webinars, office hours, and educational sessions.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Direct customers/prospects to resources (help docs, videos, automations) that resolve blockers without requiring 1:1 intervention.</span></li> </ul> </li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong><strong>Deliver Targeted Customer Interventions</strong></strong></span> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Identify accounts showing clear setup friction or usage blockers and step in with qualified, high-value interventions.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Drive trial-to-paid conversions where PGP guidance removes obstacles and enables customers to commit to a paid plan.</span></li> </ul> </li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong><strong>Run Many-to-1 Programs</strong></strong></span> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Design, host, and optimize scalable programs (hands-on labs, “best practice” webinars, AMA sessions) that drive activation, adoption, and expansion at scale.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Measure program reach, engagement quality, and downstream business impact (trial conversion, connector expansion, MAR growth).</span></li> </ul> </li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong><strong>Enable Expansion Readiness</strong></strong></span> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Identify accounts with expansion potential (new connectors, workloads, or use cases) and surface them for sales or CSM follow-up when appropriate.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Share preventative guidance to reduce drop-offs and ensure smooth adoption as accounts scale.</span></li> </ul> </li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong><strong>Advocate for Customers</strong></strong></span> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Capture customer insights and funnel them back to product, engineering, and marketing teams to improve self-service experiences.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Build scalable resources (guides, templates, content) that multiply across the customer base.</span></li> </ul> </li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong><strong>Handle Reactive Needs</strong></strong></span> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Support “lights-on” requests (low-touch commercial tasks, small contract adjustments, billing/account escalations) efficiently and effectively.</span><br><span style="font-size: 10pt;">&nbsp;</span></li> </ul> </li> </ul> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Skills We're Looking For</strong></span></p> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Experience: 1–2 years in customer success, product support, enablement, or similar role.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Customer-Centric Mindset: Ability to diagnose customer friction points and provide clear, scalable solutions.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Technical Proficiency: Foundational knowledge in SQL, APIs, cloud data warehouses (Snowflake, Databricks, BigQuery, Redshift), and basic data ELT concepts.&nbsp;</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Program Builder: Experience running group enablement (webinars, labs, training, or community events) preferred.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Analytical Skills: Comfortable using CRM and product analytics to identify usage patterns, triggers, and opportunities.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Collaboration: Proven ability to partner with cross-functional teams (product, sales, support, RevOps) to improve customer outcomes.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Communication: Strong written and verbal communication skills for both 1:1 and many-to-1 interactions.</span></li> <li style="font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Adaptability: Thrives in an environment where success is measured by customer outcomes and scalable impact, not quota attainment</span><span style="font-size: 10pt;">.</span></li> </ul> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">#LI-Hybrid #LI-LW1</span></p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p>The compensation range displayed on this job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire compensation for the target position and level, and may include sales incentives or target bonuses depending on the role. &nbsp;Our compensation ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Our job titles may span more than one career level. Within the range, individual compensation is determined by additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, relevant education or training, business need, market demands. The compensation range is subject to change and may be modified in the future. Your recruiter can share more about the specific compensation range for your location during the hiring process.</p></div><div class="title">Denver Pay Range</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$94,496</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$113,400 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p>&nbsp;</p> <p class="p-rich_text_section" style="line-height: 1.3;"><strong data-stringify-type="bold">Perks and Benefits</strong></p> <ul> <li>100% employer-paid medical insurance<strong>*</strong></li> <li>Generous paid time-off policy (PTO), plus paid sick time, inclusive parental leave policy, holidays, and volunteer days off</li> <li>RSU stock grants*</li> <li>Professional development and training opportunities</li> <li>Company virtual happy hours, free food, and fun team-building activities</li> <li>Monthly cell phone stipend</li> <li>Access to an innovative mental health support platform that offers personalized care and resources in areas such as: therapy, coaching, and self-guided mindfulness exercises for all covered employees and their covered dependents.</li> </ul> <p style="line-height: 1.3;"><em><strong>*</strong>May vary by country and worker type - please reach out to your recruiter for more information</em></p> <p style="line-height: 1.3;"><em>Click <a href="https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1CdawUwjo1Q1B7ghZJ_owdmmHB8VtqhBu?usp=sharing" target="_blank">here</a> to learn more about Fivetran's Benefits by Region.</em></p> <hr> <p style="line-height: 1.3;">We’re honored to be <a class="c-link" href="https://fivetran.com/blog/hvr-acquisition-series-d" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://fivetran.com/blog/hvr-acquisition-series-d" data-sk="tooltip_parent">valued at over $5.6 billion</a>, but more importantly, we’re proud of our&nbsp;<a class="c-link" href="https://fivetran.com/culture" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://fivetran.com/culture" data-sk="tooltip_parent">core values of Get Stuck In, Do the Right Thing, and One Team, One Dream</a>. Read about us in&nbsp;<a class="c-link" href="https://fivetran-com.s3.amazonaws.com/news/forbes-aug-sept-2022-digital-reprint-10-21-22.pdf" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://fivetran-com.s3.amazonaws.com/news/forbes-aug-sept-2022-digital-reprint-10-21-22.pdf" data-sk="tooltip_parent">Forbes</a>.&nbsp; &nbsp; &nbsp;</p> <p style="line-height: 1.3;">Fivetran brings together high-quality talent across the globe to make data access as easy and reliable as electricity for our customers. We value and recognize that our customers benefit from having innovative teams made of people from many backgrounds, experiences, and identities. Fivetran promotes diversity, equity, inclusion &amp; belonging through attracting, recruiting, developing, and retaining a diverse workforce, not only because it is the right thing to do, but because it helps us build a world-class company to better serve our customers, our people and our communities.</p> <p style="line-height: 1.3;">To learn more about Fivetran’s culture and what it’s like to be part of the team, <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xlhtp4dGh8o" target="_blank">click here</a> and enjoy our video.</p> <p style="line-height: 1.3;">To learn more about our candidate privacy policy, you can <a href="https://fivetran.com/candidate-privacy" target="_blank">read our statement here</a>.</p> <hr> <p style="line-height: 1.3;"><em>We are committed to ensuring that all candidates have an equal opportunity to participate in our interview process. We will work with you to provide reasonable accommodations to facilitate your participation and ensure a fair and accessible interview experience. Your request and any information provided will be kept confidential and will not impact your candidacy. We look forward to hearing from you and accommodating your needs to the best of our ability.</em></p></div>

jobs.title: Customer Success Associate
jobs.description: <p>As a Customer Success Associate, you will&nbsp; focus on the long term strategic relationship with our customers, drive adoption, and identify growth opportunities across a high volume of Datadog customers to ensure successful product use and retention. You’ll be an advocate for the customer internally and focus on providing a positive experience for a high volume of accounts. Associates follow a well-defined methodology to identify the customer’s unique needs and clearly convey the value of the Datadog product. As we scale our Customer Success team, furthering personal development and team success is a top priority.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Identify target opportunities, manage the full sales cycle and proactively build strategic relationships with customers and focus on retention.&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> <li>Manage requests from your book of business such as but not limited to technical support escalations, billing questions, documentation/enablement requests to ensure a positive customer experience</li> <li>Pivot account management strategy based on different customer requirements and needs seamlessly and efficiently</li> <li>Act as an advisor to customers to ensure they’re leveraging the solution effectively</li> <li>Monitor and identify usage trends to uncover renewal risks and support greater adoption rates</li> <li>Collaborate cross-functionally with internal Datadog teams (support, product, finance, and legal)</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Customer-minded, always trying to provide the most value possible</li> <li>Driven and motivated by a career in sales with a strong desire to grow a career in Customer Success.</li> <li>A strong communicator (written and verbal) with excellent attention to detail</li> <li>Experienced in independently negotiating contract terms and overcoming pricing objections for up-sell/cross sell activities</li> <li>0-2 years of experience in a customer account facing role and desire to grow a career in Customer Success</li> <li>Passionate about building long term and lasting relationships</li> </ul> <p style="color: white;">&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.</em></p> <p style="color: white;">&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Benefits and Growth:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Best-in-breed onboarding</li> <li>Sales training in MEDDIC and Command of the Message&nbsp;</li> <li>An inclusive company culture, opportunity to join our Community Guilds</li> <li>Intra-departmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking&nbsp;</li> <li>Continuous professional development, product training, and career pathing&nbsp;</li> <li>New hire stock equity (RSU) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)</li> <li>Generous global benefits</li> </ul> <p style="color: white;">&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Hybrid</p><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. We are on a mission to break down silos and solve complexity in the cloud age by enabling digital transformation, cloud migration, and infrastructure monitoring of our customers’ entire technology stacks. Built by engineers, for engineers, Datadog is used by organizations of all sizes across a wide range of industries. Together, we champion professional development, diversity of thought, innovation, and work excellence to empower continuous growth. Join the pack and become part of a collaborative, pragmatic, and thoughtful people-first community where we solve tough problems, take smart risks, and celebrate one another. Learn more about #DatadogLife on <a href="https://www.instagram.com/datadoghq/?hl=en" target="_blank">Instagram</a>, <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/company/datadog" target="_blank">LinkedIn,</a> and <a href="https://learn.datadoghq.com/" target="_blank">Datadog Learning Center. </a></p> <hr> <p data-renderer-start-pos="215"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Equal Opportunity at Datadog:</strong></p> <p>Datadog is proud to offer <a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf" target="_blank">equal employment opportunity</a> to everyone regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, veteran status, and other characteristics protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. Here are our <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/candidate-legal-notices/" target="_blank">Candidate Legal Notices</a> for your reference.&nbsp;</p> <p>Datadog endeavors to make our Careers Page accessible to all users. If you would like to contact us regarding the accessibility of our website or need assistance completing the application process, please complete <a href="https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeoJeduEZ2NdZF_65uZmWUrCM-zee7yADXX1s6Xr9zAGq8iKA/viewform" target="_blank">this form</a>. This form is for accommodation requests only and cannot be used to inquire about the status of applications.&nbsp;</p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="662"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Privacy and AI Guidelines:</strong></p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="662">Any information you submit to Datadog as part of your application will be processed in accordance with Datadog’s&nbsp;<a class="c-link" href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/applicant-candidate-privacy/" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/applicant-candidate-privacy/" data-sk="tooltip_parent">Applicant and Candidate Privacy Notice</a>. For information on our AI policy, please visit <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/interviewing-at-datadog-ai-guidelines/" target="_blank">Interviewing at Datadog AI Guidelines</a>.</p></div>

jobs.title: GTM Strategy/Operations Associate - Dublin
jobs.description: <p>Datadog’s EMEA GTM Strategy &amp; Operations team is responsible for both strategic planning and day‑to‑day operational excellence across the region. The team uncovers, analyzes, and prioritizes improvement opportunities that drive incremental revenue and operational efficiency. We build business cases for EMEA investment, optimize resources during annual planning, and collaborate closely with global teams to enhance systems, tools, programs, and processes. We also lead the execution of global initiatives within EMEA and share best practices across regions to accelerate global scale.</p> <p>As part of the EMEA GTM Strategy &amp; Operations team, you’ll work with sales leaders and global partners to improve how we plan, execute, and automate core GTM processes across the region. You’ll be AI‑native in how you work, using modern AI tools to get to insight faster, diagnose root causes, and free your time for driving change and partnering with the business.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture – the relationships and collaboration it builds, and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work‑life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do</strong></p> <ul> <li>Build strong relationships with local, regional, and global cross‑functional stakeholders to enhance GTM strategy and operations for EMEA, sharing learnings and best practices across markets.</li> <li>Localize and execute global GTM policies and sales programs (for example: pipeline generation programs, product plays, territory deep dives), ensuring they are adapted effectively to EMEA market realities.</li> <li>Act as a primary point of contact for a defined set of sales leaders on strategic and operational questions (e.g. account coverage, rules of engagement, data led operating cadence, QBR inputs etc..).</li> <li>Orchestrate and support the rhythm of business, including data preparation and standardization for Quarterly Business Reviews (QBRs), forecast/LQSR cadences, and annual planning cycles.</li> <li>Partner with systems, data, and central operations teams to simplify and automate recurring work (for example: standard QBR/LQSR kits, self‑serve lists and dashboards, queue and SLA reporting), using AI‑enabled workflows where it meaningfully reduces manual effort.</li> <li>Produce analyses, reports, and recommendations on sales performance, coverage, pipeline, and program effectiveness, leveraging AI tools to explore data faster, spot patterns, and get to root causes so you can focus more time on driving action with stakeholders.</li> <li>Manage cross‑functional projects from initiation to completion, ensuring alignment with strategic objectives, clear timelines, and well‑defined success metrics.</li> <li>Document processes, definitions, and best practices so that outputs are scalable, repeatable, and easy for sales leaders and partner teams to adopt.</li> </ul> <p>Who You Are</p> <ul> <li>2–4+ years of experience in GTM Strategy &amp; Operations, management consulting, banking, analytics, or a similar analytical field, ideally within SaaS or high‑growth B2B technology.</li> <li>Experience working with go‑to‑market teams (e.g. Sales, CS, Marketing) and comfort acting as a trusted advisor to commercial leadership.</li> <li>Strong analytical skills and ability to translate data into clear insights and practical recommendations that improve business performance.</li> <li>Comfort using modern AI tools as part of your day‑to‑day workflow (for example, to accelerate analysis, draft and refine narratives, or prototype simple automations), and curiosity to experiment with new capabilities.</li> <li>Familiarity with Salesforce and BI tools (e.g. Tableau, Metabase, or similar); experience with SQL or a willingness to learn on the job.</li> <li>High attention to detail, strong organizational skills, and the ability to manage multiple priorities and stakeholders in a fast‑moving environment.</li> <li>Comfortable operating in ambiguity and shifting between big‑picture strategic thinking and hands‑on execution.</li> <li>Effective verbal and written communicator who can build alignment and influence cross‑functional partners.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-NG2</p><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. This engineering leader will guide a team responsible for the query path for traces and spans search that powers the Datadog UI, while also leading the evolution of the APM MCP Toolset that enables agentic workflows with access to APM telemetry and intelligence, including metrics, spans, traces, service health, and related capabilities. This role offers the opportunity to lead a future-facing API platform at the intersection of distributed systems, developer experience, and AI-powered product development. The person in this role will help define how Datadog exposes APM intelligence across MCP tools, skills, CLI interfaces, and other emerging surfaces as the ecosystem evolves.<br><br></p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table.
