jobs.title: Software Engineer 3, Atlas Clusters
jobs.description: <p>MongoDB is seeking a Software Engineer to join the Atlas Clusters Organization. The organization is responsible for building MongoDB Atlas, our database as a service offering and fastest growing product.<strong> </strong>Atlas allows users to deploy fault-tolerant, secure, globally distributed MongoDB clusters in just minutes. This includes developing software to interface with the three major cloud providers (AWS, Azure, and GCP) in order to bring security, durability, availability, and performance to all deployments of MongoDB.</p> <p>We are forming a new Atlas Clusters team in the Dublin area. We are looking to speak to candidates who are based in Dublin and would like a hybrid or in-office working model.</p> <h3>What you’ll do</h3> <ul> <li>Build and design new features for MongoDB Atlas</li> <li>Contribute to and lead complex technical projects</li> <li>Work with stakeholders throughout MongoDB to build our roadmap and product offerings</li> <li>Work with customers and support engineers to fix issues and become part of our on-call rotation&nbsp;</li> <li>Collaborate with team members to develop our codebase, best practices, and design principles</li> <li>Foster an inclusive and respectful work environment according to MongoDB's Core Values</li> </ul> <h3>We’re looking for someone who</h3> <ul> <li>Has at least 3 years of professional software development experience</li> <li>Is skilled at writing large-scale, distributed backend systems in a compiled language (Go, Java, C#, etc.).&nbsp;</li> <li>Has experience with at least one major cloud provider technology (AWS, Azure, GCP)</li> <li>Has led the launch of a new critical feature and maintained it in production</li> <li>Is eager to solve tough problems</li> <li>Has excellent communication skills</li> <li>Is curious, collaborative, and motivated</li> </ul> <h3>Success Measures</h3> <ul> <li>In 3 months, you'll have shipped code into production and collaborated with the team to solve tough problems</li> <li>In 6 months, you'll have contributed to a large project&nbsp;</li> <li>In 12 months, you'll have designed new features, led development work, and become a go-to expert on parts of the system</li> </ul> <h3>About MongoDB</h3> <p>MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the database for the AI era, enabling innovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. MongoDB’s unified database platform—the most widely available, globally distributed database on the market—helps organizations modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our cloud-native platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi-cloud database and is available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.</p> <p>With offices worldwide and nearly 60,000 customers—including 75% of the Fortune 100 and AI-native startups—relying on MongoDB for their most important applications, we’re powering the next era of software.</p> <p>Our compass at MongoDB is our <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company">Leadership Commitment,</a> guiding how and why we make decisions, show up for each other, and win. It’s what makes us MongoDB.&nbsp;</p> <p>To drive the personal growth and business impact of our employees, we’re committed to developing a supportive and enriching culture for everyone. <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company/blog/culture/employee-benefits-that-make-a-difference-at-mongodb">From employee affinity groups, to fertility assistance and a generous parental leave policy</a>, we value our employees’ wellbeing and want to support them along every step of their professional and personal journeys.<a href="https://www.mongodb.com/blog/channel/culture"> Learn more about what it’s like to work at MongoDB</a>, and help us make an impact on the world!</p> <p>MongoDB is committed to providing any necessary accommodations for individuals with disabilities within our application and interview process. To request an accommodation due to a disability, please inform your recruiter.</p> <p>MongoDB is an equal opportunities employer.</p> <p>REQ ID: 426096</p>

jobs.title: Software Engineer (Internal Query Engine Testing Tools), Query
jobs.description: <p>The MongoDB Query Testing Tools Team is hiring Software Engineers who want to join us in building a state-of-the-art distributed query system. Our Team builds tooling that allows us to ship Query Engine features with high confidence in both correctness and performance, making it a great fit if you like seeing your work protect real customers at scale. The team develops advanced testing frameworks (end-to-end testing, fuzzing, property-based testing) as well as performance measurement tools, including high-volume workload generation tools.</p> <p>The team is part of the Query organization which is responsible for the MongoDB Query Language and the lifecycle of each query, from parsing to optimization to plan selection; our engineers work on maintaining and improving our optimization and execution frameworks and building new query language features to help power our customers' critical workloads. Throughout all our work, our highest priority is always ensuring that customer queries are answered correctly. The successful applicant will be responsible for helping us to maintain the highest standards of quality through rigorous development, testing and automation strategies.</p> <p>This role can be based out of our Dublin office, or remotely in the Ireland region.</p> <h3>Candidate Profile</h3> <ul> <li>3+ years of experience in software development and/or performance engineering.</li> <li>Fluency in Python, Javascript or Typescript</li> <li>Enjoys chasing down difficult problems in a distributed environment</li> <li>Able to make complex concepts easy to grasp in written and spoken English</li> <li>Able to decompose and solve complex problems and can rapidly iterate by validating new hypotheses and rethinking current assumptions</li> <li>Demonstrating hands-on experience working with relational or NoSQL databases is a plus</li> <li>B.Sc. in Computer Science or related field, or equivalent practical experience. Advanced degrees of experience in database query engines is a plus</li> <li>Experience in developing automation test frameworks is a plus.</li> <li>Experience in query processing engine testing is a strong plus.</li> <li>Experience in developing performance test plans and monitoring frameworks is a strong plus.</li> <li>Background in database query engines or compilers is a strong plus.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h3>Position Expectations</h3> <ul> <li>Design, develop, and maintain automated testing frameworks, Performance automated tests and internal tooling for the MongoDB query engine</li> <li>Collaborate closely with peers to understand new features and functionalities, ensuring testability, test frameworks, tooling and coverage for automating tests of both happy-path and edge-case scenarios.</li> <li>Provide feedback in code reviews, focusing on the robustness and reliability of code changes.</li> </ul> <h3>Success Measures</h3> <ul> <li>Within three months, you have gained an understanding of MongoDB's MQL syntax and its semantics, as well as the behavior of our unique query engine. You have contributed to several code bases on Mongodb engine internal tooling.&nbsp;</li> <li>By six months, you have established yourself as a key owner of automated test frameworks, and demonstrated improvements in test efficiency and coverage.&nbsp;</li> <li>After twelve months, you are instrumental in ensuring quality of major new features, setting an example for new team members in best practices for correctness and performance test automation and internal tooling.</li> </ul> <h3>About MongoDB</h3> <p>MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the database for the AI era, enabling innovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. MongoDB’s unified database platform, the most widely available, globally distributed database on the market, helps organizations modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our cloud-native platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi-cloud database and is available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.</p> <p>With offices worldwide and over 60,000 customers, including 75% of the Fortune 100 and AI-native startups, relying on MongoDB for their most important applications, we’re powering the next era of software.</p> <p>Our compass at MongoDB is our <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company">Leadership Commitment,</a> guiding how and why we make decisions, show up for each other, and win. It’s what makes us MongoDB.&nbsp;</p> <p>To drive the personal growth and business impact of our employees, we’re committed to developing a supportive and enriching culture for everyone. <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company/blog/culture/employee-benefits-that-make-a-difference-at-mongodb">From employee affinity groups, to fertility assistance and a generous parental leave policy</a>, we value our employees’ wellbeing and want to support them along every step of their professional and personal journeys.<a href="https://www.mongodb.com/blog/channel/culture"> Learn more about what it’s like to work at MongoDB</a>, and help us make an impact on the world!</p> <p>MongoDB is committed to providing any necessary accommodations for individuals with disabilities within our application and interview process. To request an accommodation due to a disability, please inform your recruiter.</p> <p>MongoDB is an equal opportunities employer.</p> <p><em>Req ID: 4263348170</em></p>

jobs.title: Software Engineer, Credit Risk Platform
jobs.description: <h2><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><strong>About the role</strong></span></h2> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Chime Engineering is growing rapidly as we scale to support the financial needs of our members. We are looking for driven engineers to join our Credit Risk Platform team, where you’ll build backend services and internal tools that help Chime manage credit risk and losses across all of our credit products, including Loans, MyPay, SpotMe, and a growing pipeline of new credit products in development.</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">This team is building a shared risk platform that expands with every new product we launch. A key part of the mission is being able to onboard new credit products as a platform capability, not a reinvention of decisioning, controls, and monitoring each time. You’ll partner closely with Credit Risk, Product, Analytics, and Data Science to help risk experts iterate safely using better tooling, richer data, and clear guardrails. That includes integrating third-party data sources and building systems that are reliable, observable, and auditable, because they sit on the critical path of member experiences and credit outcomes.</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">The base salary offered for this role and level of experience will begin at $133,000 and up to $184,000. Full-time employees are also eligible for a bonus, competitive equity package, and benefits. The actual base salary offered may be higher, depending on your location, skills, qualifications, and experience.&nbsp;</span></p> <h2><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><strong>In this role, you can expect to</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Design, develop, test, and maintain backend services and internal tools that support risk strategy across multiple products (e.g., eligibility, limits, exposure tracking, policy enforcement)</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Contribute to reusable platform primitives that make it faster and safer to launch new credit products without duplicating risk decisioning and controls</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Build and improve workflows that enable Credit Risk teams to adjust strategies (rules, thresholds, segmentation) with appropriate guardrails and review processes</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Integrate third-party data sources used in decisioning and monitoring, including implementing data validation, error handling, and resilience to vendor changes</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Add instrumentation, dashboards, and alerts for key risk and loss signals, and participate in on-call/incident response as part of a supportive team rotation</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Collaborate with senior engineers to learn best practices in system design, testing, and operational excellence</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Proactively identify reliability and scalability improvements and drive them to completion with mentorship and feedback</span></li> </ul> <h2><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><strong>To thrive in this role, you have</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">3+ years developing software in a production environment</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Experience with Ruby on Rails or comparable frameworks, building and operating APIs</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Experience working with relational databases and caching systems, and writing safe schema changes and migrations</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Familiarity with distributed systems fundamentals (async processing, idempotency, retries, backfills) and an interest in learning deeper system design</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">A strong appreciation for maintainable, well-tested code and a willingness to improve existing systems, not just ship new ones</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Comfort working across teams to clarify requirements, communicate tradeoffs, and deliver incrementally</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Interest in data-driven systems and integrating external/third-party data sources (fintech or risk experience is a plus, not required)</span></li> </ul> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">#LI-Hybrid #LI-SB1</span></p><div class="content-conclusion"><h2><strong>A little about us</strong></h2> <p>At Chime, we believe that everyone can achieve financial progress. We created Chime—a financial technology company, not a bank*—on the premise that core banking services should be helpful, easy, and free. Through our user-friendly tools and intuitive platforms, we empower our members to take control of their finances and work towards their goals. Whether it's starting a savings account, purchasing a first car or home, launching a business, or pursuing higher education, we're proud to have helped millions unlock their financial potential.</p> <p>We're a team of problem solvers, dreamers, and builders with one shared obsession: our members. From day one, Chimers have worked tirelessly to out-hustle and out-execute competitors to bring our mission to life. Their grit and determination inspire us to work harder every day to deliver the very best experience possible. We each bring an owner's mindset to our work, refusing to be outdone and holding ourselves accountable to meet and exceed the highest bars for our teams, our company, and our members.</p> <p>We believe in being bold, dreaming big, and taking risks, while also working together, embracing our diverse perspectives, and giving each other honest feedback. Our culture remains deeply entrepreneurial, encouraging every Chimer to see themselves as stewards of our mission to help everyday Americans unlock their financial progress.&nbsp;</p> <p>We know that to achieve our mission, we must earn and keep people's trust—so we hold ourselves to the highest standards of integrity in everything we do. These aren't just words on a wall—our values are embedded in every aspect of our business, serving as a north star that guides us as we work to help millions achieve their financial potential.</p> <p>Because if we don't—who will?</p> <p>*Chime is a financial technology company, not a bank. Banking services provided by The Bancorp Bank, N.A. or Stride Bank, N.A., Members FDIC.</p> <h2><strong>What we offer for our full-time, regular employees</strong></h2> <ul> <li>🏢 Our in-office work policy is designed to keep you connected - with four days a week in the office and Fridays from home for those near one of our offices, plus team and company-wide events depending on location. Whether you’re coming in regularly or are part of our fully remote program, you’ll stay engaged with your work and teammates.</li> <li>💻 In-office perks including backup child, elder, and/or pet care, plus a subsidized commuter benefit to support your regular commute</li> <li>💰 Competitive salary based on experience</li> <li>✨ 401k match plus great medical, dental, vision, life, and disability benefits</li> <li>🏝 Generous vacation policy and company-wide Chime Days, bonus company-wide paid days off</li> <li>🫂 1% of your time off to support local community organizations of your choice</li> <li>👟 Annual wellness stipend to use towards eligible wellness related expenses</li> <li>👶 Up to 24 weeks of paid parental leave for birthing parents and 12 weeks of paid parental leave for non-birthing parents</li> <li>👪 Access to Maven, a family planning tool, with $15k lifetime reimbursement for egg freezing, fertility treatments, adoption, and more.</li> <li>🎉 In-person and virtual events to connect with your fellow Chimers—think cooking classes, guided meditations, music festivals, mixology classes, paint nights, etc., and delicious snack boxes, too!</li> <li>💚 A challenging and fulfilling opportunity to join one of the most experienced teams in FinTech and help millions unlock financial progress</li> </ul> <p>We know that great work can’t be done without a diverse team and inclusive environment. That’s why we specifically look for individuals of varying strengths, skills, backgrounds, and ideas to join our team. We believe this gives us a competitive advantage to better serve our members and helps us all grow as Chimers and individuals.</p> <p>Chime is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We consider qualified applicants without regard to race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, marital or family status, disability, genetic information, veteran status, or any other legally protected basis under provincial, federal, state, and local laws, regulations, or ordinances. We will also consider qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with the requirements of state and local laws, including the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, Cook County Ordinance, NYC Fair Chance Act, and the LA City Fair Chance Ordinance, and consistent with Canadian provincial and federal laws. jobs.description: <h2><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><strong>About the role</strong></span></h2> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">We are looking for a Software Engineer to help build our new financial platform that is highly reliable, scalable, and will serve as the backbone for Chime. This platform will power the most important financial activities that millions of Chime members undertake every day. You will ensure that we build the highest levels of trust with our members by making our platform lightning-fast, available, secure, resilient and auditable. This is an exciting opportunity to be an invaluable member of a strategically important&nbsp; initiative for Chime.&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">The base salary offered for this role and level of experience will begin at $133,000 and up to $184,000. Full-time employees are also eligible for a bonus, competitive equity package, and benefits. The actual base salary offered may be higher, depending on your location, skills, qualifications, and experience.</span></p> <h2><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><strong>In this role, you can expect to</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Design, develop, test, and deploy components of a new financial platform</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Deliver secure, reliable, and high-performance infrastructure and APIs</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Work with our infrastructure engineering team to minimize the operational overhead of production systems you deploy</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Develop thoroughly tested code and conduct quality assurance on all new code alongside our QA team</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Learn and execute on agile methodologies, follow test driven development approach</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Engineers are required to participate in on-call rotation. Being on call may include responding to incidents outside of regular working hours when necessary.</span></li> </ul> <h2><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><strong>To thrive in this role, you have</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Must have experience in Go</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Experience in additional backend languages such as Ruby, Java and Python is preferred</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Have 4+ years of engineering experience</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Have contributed to the development of backend systems</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Experience in payment processing systems</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Take a rigorous approach to testing backed development</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Experience with AWS or other cloud platforms, Cloud Native technology, No-SQL Databases as a bonus!</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Have an understanding of architectural patterns of large, mission-critical production services as a bonus!</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Have worked in continuous integration/deployment environments as a bonus!</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Please note: If you are located in Seattle, you will be required to onboard in person at our SF HQ + occasional business travel.</span></li> </ul> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">#LI-Onsite #LI-GC1</span></p><div class="content-conclusion"><h2><strong>A little about us</strong></h2> <p>At Chime, we believe that everyone can achieve financial progress. We created Chime—a financial technology company, not a bank*—on the premise that core banking services should be helpful, easy, and free. Through our user-friendly tools and intuitive platforms, we empower our members to take control of their finances and work towards their goals. Whether it's starting a savings account, purchasing a first car or home, launching a business, or pursuing higher education, we're proud to have helped millions unlock their financial potential.</p> <p>We're a team of problem solvers, dreamers, and builders with one shared obsession: our members. From day one, Chimers have worked tirelessly to out-hustle and out-execute competitors to bring our mission to life. Their grit and determination inspire us to work harder every day to deliver the very best experience possible. We each bring an owner's mindset to our work, refusing to be outdone and holding ourselves accountable to meet and exceed the highest bars for our teams, our company, and our members.</p> <p>We believe in being bold, dreaming big, and taking risks, while also working together, embracing our diverse perspectives, and giving each other honest feedback. Our culture remains deeply entrepreneurial, encouraging every Chimer to see themselves as stewards of our mission to help everyday Americans unlock their financial progress.&nbsp;</p> <p>We know that to achieve our mission, we must earn and keep people's trust—so we hold ourselves to the highest standards of integrity in everything we do. These aren't just words on a wall—our values are embedded in every aspect of our business, serving as a north star that guides us as we work to help millions achieve their financial potential.</p> <p>Because if we don't—who will?</p> <p>*Chime is a financial technology company, not a bank. Banking services provided by The Bancorp Bank, N.A. or Stride Bank, N.A., Members FDIC.</p> <h2><strong>What we offer for our full-time, regular employees</strong></h2> <ul> <li>🏢 Our in-office work policy is designed to keep you connected - with four days a week in the office and Fridays from home for those near one of our offices, plus team and company-wide events depending on location. Whether you’re coming in regularly or are part of our fully remote program, you’ll stay engaged with your work and teammates.</li> <li>💻 In-office perks including backup child, elder, and/or pet care, plus a subsidized commuter benefit to support your regular commute</li> <li>💰 Competitive salary based on experience</li> <li>✨ 401k match plus great medical, dental, vision, life, and disability benefits</li> <li>🏝 Generous vacation policy and company-wide Chime Days, bonus company-wide paid days off</li> <li>🫂 1% of your time off to support local community organizations of your choice</li> <li>👟 Annual wellness stipend to use towards eligible wellness related expenses</li> <li>👶 Up to 24 weeks of paid parental leave for birthing parents and 12 weeks of paid parental leave for non-birthing parents</li> <li>👪 Access to Maven, a family planning tool, with $15k lifetime reimbursement for egg freezing, fertility treatments, adoption, and more.</li> <li>🎉 In-person and virtual events to connect with your fellow Chimers—think cooking classes, guided meditations, music festivals, mixology classes, paint nights, etc., and delicious snack boxes, too!</li> <li>💚 A challenging and fulfilling opportunity to join one of the most experienced teams in FinTech and help millions unlock financial progress</li> </ul> <p>We know that great work can’t be done without a diverse team and inclusive environment. That’s why we specifically look for individuals of varying strengths, skills, backgrounds, and ideas to join our team. We believe this gives us a competitive advantage to better serve our members and helps us all grow as Chimers and individuals.</p> <p>Chime is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We consider qualified applicants without regard to race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, marital or family status, disability, genetic information, veteran status, or any other legally protected basis under provincial, federal, state, and local laws, regulations, or ordinances. We will also consider qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with the requirements of state and local laws, including the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, Cook County Ordinance, NYC Fair Chance Act, and the LA City Fair Chance Ordinance, and consistent with Canadian provincial and federal laws. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation during any stage of the application process, please contact: <a href="mailto:benefits@chime.com">benefits@chime.com</a>.</p> <p>To learn more about how Chime collects and uses your personal information during the application process, please see the&nbsp;<a href="https://www.chime.com/careers/chime-applicant-privacy-notice/">Chime Applicant Privacy Notice. </a></p></div>

jobs.title: Business Development Representative, Enterprise - Spanish Speaker
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p>From Fivetran’s founding until now, our mission has remained the same: to make access to data as simple and reliable as electricity. With Fivetran, customer data arrives in their warehouses, canonical and ready to query, with no engineering or maintenance required. We’re proud that more organizations continue to leverage our technology every day to become truly data-driven.</p></div><p><strong><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">About the Role</span></strong></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">We’re looking for an Enterprise Business Development Representative to join our team! In this role you will work closely with your Account Executives, Marketing and Partner teams to ensure EMEA builds strong opportunities in key target accounts. jobs.title: Software Engineer II - Oracle Databases
jobs.location: Novi Sad, South Bačka, Serbia, EMEA
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p>From Fivetran’s founding until now, our mission has remained the same: to make access to data as simple and reliable as electricity. With Fivetran, customer data arrives in their warehouses, canonical and ready to query, with no engineering or maintenance required. We’re proud that more organizations continue to leverage our technology every day to become truly data-driven.</p></div><p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><strong>About the Role</strong></span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Fivetran is building data pipelines to power the modern data stack for thousands of companies.</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">We are looking for a Software Engineer enthusiastic to join our fast-growing company.&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">As a part of the Oracle Connector Team, you will be responsible to help build new features of our data pipeline service, help improve our software architecture, and maintain reliable operation of the service. We're looking for someone who wants to learn and apply the key values that make our product great, dive deep into complex investigations into data replication and build a world class ELT solution for Oracle systems.</span><br><br><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Fivetran is the epitome of data-driven-development — our engineering team is focused on building a world class product that:</span><br><br><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">- Facilitates Data Analytics — join our mission to reliably deliver best-in class schemas, and clean data, designed to be intuitively useful to analysts.</span><br><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">- Contributes to our Community — help develop tools like Data Lakes, SQL-based Transformations, and schema explorers to empower analysts to choose the best tools, and to make those tools more powerful.</span><br><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">- Is Absolutely Dependable — work to make Fivetran the most reliable and secure component of our customers’ data infrastructure.</span><br><br><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">We emphasize using no-nonsense tools, and take great pride in the simplicity and effectiveness of the systems we build. Our back-end is built on Java, C, Postgres, and Kubernetes, and our front-end is built on React and Typescript.</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">This is a full-time position based out of our Novi Sad office. Our hybrid work model offers a blend of remote flexibility and in-person collaboration, including two days in the office each week to connect and build as a team.</span></p> <p><strong><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Technologies You’ll Use</span></strong></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Java, C, Oracle, SQL, Docker/Kubernetes</span></p> <p><strong><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">What You’ll Do</span></strong></p> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Dive deep into data replication debugging, analyzing existing code that is in use by hundreds of customers today. Some clients are operating the largest Oracle clusters in the world.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Analyze new features that are introduced by Oracle in recent releases and determine how to develop supporting features.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Contribute to the efforts to the maintenance and improvement of existing connectors; call for improvement in all areas of Fivetran's codebase and infrastructure.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Be a part of the process to move a high performance client side service to the cloud and operate at scale.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Develop software designs and technical roadmap for the team.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Contribute to hiring by participating in the interview process.</span></li> </ul> <p><strong><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Skills We’re Looking For</span></strong></p> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Coding:</span> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">2+ years programming in Java or C languages, strong preference to C/C++ for this role&nbsp;</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Writes well-structured and performant code</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Understand and can explain how to build high performance systems and processes</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Have an analytical mindset that helps you identify design flaws in existing systems and come up with improvements</span></li> </ul> </li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">System:</span> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">1+ years of of working in a cloud environment utilizing AWS, GCP, Docker/Kubernetes, CircleCI, or similar technologies</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">1+ years of infrastructure as code on Docker/Kubernetes, AWS CloudFormation, Terraform, Google Cloud Deployment Manager, or equivalent.</span></li> </ul> </li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Project management:</span> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; Proficient at designing software and infrastructure</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Experience understanding customer requirements and/or problems and breaking them down into engineering tasks</span></li> </ul> </li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Experience working in large distributed teams</span></li> </ul> </li> </ul> <p><strong><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Bonus Skills​</span></strong></p> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Working experience with database internals such as storage format, compression techniques, encryption, disaster recovery, transaction management, or high availability.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Working experience in designing and developing file, event, application, or database connectors.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Working experience with advanced Java development such as multi-threads, multi-tasking, different asynchronous programming mechanisms, garbage collector tuning, etc.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">Working experience setting up, maintaining and tuning production Oracle RDBMS.</span></li> </ul> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif; font-size: 10pt;">#LI-NR1<br>#LI-HYBRID</span></p><div class="content-conclusion"><p>&nbsp;</p> <p class="p-rich_text_section" style="line-height: 1.3;"><strong data-stringify-type="bold">Perks and Benefits</strong></p> <ul> <li>100% employer-paid medical insurance<strong>*</strong></li> <li>Generous paid time-off policy (PTO), plus paid sick time, inclusive parental leave policy, holidays, and volunteer days off</li> <li>RSU stock grants*</li> <li>Professional development and training opportunities</li> <li>Company virtual happy hours, free food, and fun team-building activities</li> <li>Monthly cell phone stipend</li> <li>Access to an innovative mental health support platform that offers personalized care and resources in areas such as: therapy, coaching, and self-guided mindfulness exercises for all covered employees and their covered dependents.</li> </ul> <p style="line-height: 1.3;"><em><strong>*</strong>May vary by country and worker type - please reach out to your recruiter for more information</em></p> <p style="line-height: 1.3;"><em>Click <a href="https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1CdawUwjo1Q1B7ghZJ_owdmmHB8VtqhBu?usp=sharing" target="_blank">here</a> to learn more about Fivetran's Benefits by Region.</em></p> <hr> <p style="line-height: 1.3;">We’re honored to be <a class="c-link" href="https://fivetran.com/blog/hvr-acquisition-series-d" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://fivetran.com/blog/hvr-acquisition-series-d" data-sk="tooltip_parent">valued at over $5.6 billion</a>, but more importantly, we’re proud of our&nbsp;<a class="c-link" href="https://fivetran.com/culture" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://fivetran.com/culture" data-sk="tooltip_parent">core values of Get Stuck In, Do the Right Thing, and One Team, One Dream</a>. jobs.title: AI Research Scientist – Datadog AI Research (DAIR)
jobs.description: <p>As a Research Scientist on our team, you will partner with Research Engineers, working on fundamental research problems and collaborating with Datadog's product and engineering teams to translate research advances into products.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>Building on our track record of AI-powered solutions (e.g., <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/bits-ai-sre/">Bits AI</a>, <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/engineering/self-optimizing-system/">Bits Evolve</a>, and our <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/ai/toto-boom-unleashed/">time series foundation model</a>), Datadog AI Research tackles high-risk, high-reward problems grounded in real-world challenges in cloud observability and security.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>We are focused on two research areas:</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <ol> <li><strong>World Models for Observability</strong> -- Training multimodal foundation models that learn the joint dynamics of distributed systems across metrics, traces, logs, topology, and events. These models power advanced forecasting, anomaly detection, root cause analysis, counterfactual simulation ("what if?"), and provide a learned planning backbone for our autonomous agents.</li> <li><strong>Trained Agents for Observability </strong>-- Post-training models to operate autonomously across Datadog's domain. SRE incident response is our first target, with a clear path to code repair, security response, and infrastructure optimization. We build the simulation environments, RL training loops, and evaluation infrastructure needed to train agents that match or surpass frontier models at a fraction of the cost.</li> </ol> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What You'll Do:</strong></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Conduct research in generative AI and machine learning, building specialized foundation models and trained agents for observability</li> <li>Train multimodal models on large-scale, diverse telemetry data (metrics, logs, traces, topology, events) using distributed training infrastructure</li> <li>Design and build simulated environments and RL training loops for on-policy agent training and evaluation</li> <li>Collaborate with cross-functional teams (Product, Engineering) to integrate capabilities like multimodal world modeling and autonomous agents into Datadog's products</li> <li>Stay at the forefront of foundation models, world models, and RL-based agent research</li> <li>Contribute to research publications, present at top-tier conferences (e.g., NeurIPS, ICLR, ICML), and help open-source key model artifacts and benchmarks</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>You hold a PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, or a related field, with deep expertise in areas like generative modeling, world models, AI agents, reinforcement learning, or multimodal learning (or have equivalent experience)</li> <li>You have extensive experience designing and implementing deep learning models and agents, with a strong background in distributed training frameworks (e.g., DeepSpeed, Megatron-LM) and ML libraries (PyTorch)</li> <li>You have a track record of impactful publications at top-tier venues (e.g., NeurIPS, ICLR, ICML, TMLR)</li> <li>You are familiar with efficient training, post-training, and inference techniques for large foundation models</li> <li>You can explain complex models and research findings to both technical and non-technical audiences</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Bonus Points (any of the following):</strong></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Experience bridging research and real-world product applications, especially with large foundation models, world models, or RL-trained agents</li> <li>Passion for pushing the boundaries of AI with a focus on customer impact and scalable deployment</li> <li>Experience writing production data pipelines and applications</li> <li>Hands-on experience with GPU programming and optimization, including CUDA</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. jobs.title: AI Research Scientist – Datadog AI Research (DAIR)
jobs.description: <p>As a Research Scientist on our team, you will partner with Research Engineers, working on fundamental research problems and collaborating with Datadog's product and engineering teams to translate research advances into products.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>Building on our track record of AI-powered solutions (e.g., <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/bits-ai-sre/">Bits AI</a>, <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/engineering/self-optimizing-system/">Bits Evolve</a>, and our <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/ai/toto-boom-unleashed/">time series foundation model</a>), Datadog AI Research tackles high-risk, high-reward problems grounded in real-world challenges in cloud observability and security.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>We are focused on two research areas:</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <ol> <li><strong>World Models for Observability</strong> -- Training multimodal foundation models that learn the joint dynamics of distributed systems across metrics, traces, logs, topology, and events. These models power advanced forecasting, anomaly detection, root cause analysis, counterfactual simulation ("what if?"), and provide a learned planning backbone for our autonomous agents.</li> <li><strong>Trained Agents for Observability </strong>-- Post-training models to operate autonomously across Datadog's domain. SRE incident response is our first target, with a clear path to code repair, security response, and infrastructure optimization. We build the simulation environments, RL training loops, and evaluation infrastructure needed to train agents that match or surpass frontier models at a fraction of the cost.</li> </ol> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What You'll Do:</strong></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Conduct research in generative AI and machine learning, building specialized foundation models and trained agents for observability</li> <li>Train multimodal models on large-scale, diverse telemetry data (metrics, logs, traces, topology, events) using distributed training infrastructure</li> <li>Design and build simulated environments and RL training loops for on-policy agent training and evaluation</li> <li>Collaborate with cross-functional teams (Product, Engineering) to integrate capabilities like multimodal world modeling and autonomous agents into Datadog's products</li> <li>Stay at the forefront of foundation models, world models, and RL-based agent research</li> <li>Contribute to research publications, present at top-tier conferences (e.g., NeurIPS, ICLR, ICML), and help open-source key model artifacts and benchmarks</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>You hold a PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, or a related field, with deep expertise in areas like generative modeling, world models, AI agents, reinforcement learning, or multimodal learning (or have equivalent experience)</li> <li>You have extensive experience designing and implementing deep learning models and agents, with a strong background in distributed training frameworks (e.g., DeepSpeed, Megatron-LM) and ML libraries (PyTorch)</li> <li>You have a track record of impactful publications at top-tier venues (e.g., NeurIPS, ICLR, ICML, TMLR)</li> <li>You are familiar with efficient training, post-training, and inference techniques for large foundation models</li> <li>You can explain complex models and research findings to both technical and non-technical audiences</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Bonus Points (any of the following):</strong></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Experience bridging research and real-world product applications, especially with large foundation models, world models, or RL-trained agents</li> <li>Passion for pushing the boundaries of AI with a focus on customer impact and scalable deployment</li> <li>Experience writing production data pipelines and applications</li> <li>Hands-on experience with GPU programming and optimization, including CUDA</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Benefits and Growth:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Competitive global benefits&nbsp;</li> <li>New hire stock equity (RSUs) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)</li> <li>Opportunity to collaborate closely with colleagues across the Datadog offices in New York City and Paris</li> <li>Opportunity to attend and present at conferences and meetups</li> <li>Intra-departmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking</li> <li>An inclusive company culture, ability to join our Community Guilds (Datadog employee resource groups)</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. We are on a mission to break down silos and solve complexity in the cloud age by enabling digital transformation, cloud migration, and infrastructure monitoring of our customers’ entire technology stacks. Together, we champion professional development, diversity of thought, innovation, and work excellence to empower continuous growth. Join the pack and become part of a collaborative, pragmatic, and thoughtful people-first community where we solve tough problems, take smart risks, and celebrate one another. Learn more about #DatadogLife on Instagram, LinkedIn, and Datadog Learning Center.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Equal Opportunity at Datadog:</strong></p> <p>Datadog is an Affirmative Action and Equal Opportunity Employer and is proud to offer equal employment opportunity to everyone regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, veteran status, and more. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. Here are our Candidate Legal Notices for your reference.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Your Privacy:</strong></p> <p>Any information you submit to Datadog as part of your application will be processed in accordance with Datadog’s Applicant and Candidate Privacy Notice.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p>Datadog offers a competitive salary and equity package, and may include variable compensation. jobs.title: Commercial Account Executive UKI/Nordics
jobs.description: <p>As an Account Executive (AE) on our Commercial Sales team, you will assist in Datadog’s overall business growth by strategically engaging and closing net-new customers across small to midsize markets. Sellers follow a well-defined methodology, collaborate with internal stakeholders, identify the customer's unique needs, and clearly convey the value of the Datadog product. AEs have the opportunity to grow their careers in Sales and continue contributing to Datadog team success.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Focus on net-new logo acquisition via outbound activity</li> <li>Become a Datadog expert through continued product and sales trainings</li> <li>Manage the full sales cycle, including technical demonstrations and negotiation&nbsp;</li> <li>Collaborate with Sales Development Representatives to drive top of funnel activity</li> <li>Strategically prospect into Chief Technology Officers, Engineering/IT Leaders, and technical end-users</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Curious, driven, and motivated as a sales person</li> <li>Creative in how you map and break into accounts</li> <li>Able to learn from feedback and champion a growth mindset</li> <li>Comfortable operating in a highly technical, fast paced environment</li> <li>Experienced in carrying quota, with a proven track record of success</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your experience, we encourage you to apply.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Benefits and Growth:</strong></p> <ul> <li>High income earning opportunities based on self performance</li> <li>New hire stock equity (RSU) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)</li> <li>Continuous professional development, product training, and career pathing&nbsp;</li> <li>Sales training in MEDDIC and Command of the Message&nbsp;</li> <li>Intra-departmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking&nbsp;</li> <li>An inclusive company culture, opportunity to join our Community Guilds</li> <li>Generous global benefits</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Hybrid</p><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. jobs.title: Commercial & Mid Market Sales Engineer
jobs.description: <p>Datadog Sales Engineers help qualify and close opportunities with customers and partners. You will provide technical expertise through sales presentations, product demonstrations, and supporting technical evaluations (POCs). Sales Engineers have a voice with the product team to help prioritize features based on input from customers, competitors, and partners. Additionally, you will work with various teams to resolve customer concerns, escalate bug issues, and serve as an ambassador for our brand. If you want to join a friendly, passionate team with limitless potential, we’d love to meet you!</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Partner with the sales team to articulate the overall Datadog value proposition, vision and strategy to customers</li> <li>Continually learn new technology to build competitive knowledge, technical skill, and credibility</li> <li>Deliver product and technical presentations with potential clients</li> <li>Have a direct line of communication with the product team to collaborate on feature requests</li> <li>Help clients onboard the product and assist when they run into roadblocks.&nbsp;</li> <li>Think creatively about a wide variety of technical challenges during the pre-sales life cycle&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Knowledgeable and experienced with DevOps monitoring or architecture tools.&nbsp;</li> <li>Comfortable and confident in delivering technical presentations/demos to either external customers or internal teams</li> <li>Able to think creatively about a wide variety of challenges. You're going to find new things every day</li> <li>Previously experienced in systems administrations: Windows or Linux</li> <li>Able to sit up to 4 hours, traveling to and from client sites&nbsp;</li> <li>Able to travel via auto, train or air up to 25% of the time</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. jobs.title: Customer Success Manager - Spanish
jobs.description: <p>As a Customer Success Manager, you will proactively drive new product attachment and effective strong relationships across our existing customer base through on-boarding, up-selling and cross selling. You’ll advocate for the customer internally and focus on a positive customer experience. Interactions are rooted in relationship-management, first and foremost, while also advocating for growth opportunities. Customer Success Managers follow a well-defined methodology that helps them identify the customer's unique needs and clearly convey the value of the Datadog product.&nbsp;</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>Partner with the sales team to ensure smooth transition and onboarding experience</li> <li>Proactively build relationships with customers to create mutual trust&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> <li>Successfully carry-out the full-sales cycle for any growth opportunities in existing customer accounts</li> <li>Act as advisor to customers to ensure they’re leveraging the Datadog solution effectively</li> <li>Monitor and identify usage trends to uncover renewal risks and promote greater adoption rates</li> <li>Collaborate cross-functionally across internal Datadog teams (Support, Product, Finance, and Legal)</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>A strong communicator with excellent attention to detail</li> <li>Fluency in the Spanish language</li> <li>Experience within Spain/Iberia Markets</li> <li>Motivated by building long-term and long-lasting relationships</li> <li>Driven by the opportunity to make a visible impact with your work</li> <li>Customer-minded, always aiming to provide the most value possible</li> <li>Excited by working in an ever-evolving and fluid solution focused environment</li> <li>Experienced in negotiating contract terms and overcoming pricing objections without direct guidance for up-sell and cross sell activities</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your experience, we encourage you to apply.</em></p> <p><strong>Benefits and Growth:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Best-in-breed onboarding</li> <li>Sales training in MEDDIC and Command of the Message&nbsp;</li> <li>An inclusive company culture, opportunity to join our Community Guilds</li> <li>Intra-departmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking&nbsp;</li> <li>Continuous professional development, product training, and career pathing&nbsp;</li> <li>New hire stock equity (RSU) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)</li> <li>Generous global benefits</li> </ul> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. jobs.title: Enterprise Sales Engineer
jobs.description: <p>As an Enterprise Sales Engineer, you will provide technical expertise through sales presentations, product demonstrations, and supporting technical evaluations (POVs). Sales Engineers help qualify and close opportunities with customers and partners and have a voice with the product team to help prioritize features based on input from customers, competitors, and partners.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture—the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Partner with the Sales team to articulate the overall Datadog value proposition, vision and strategy to customers</li> <li>Own technical engagement with customers during the trial phase. Communicate Datadog’s value based on activities and work with customers on any identified issues or concerns to successful conclusion</li> <li>Technically close complex opportunities through advanced competitive knowledge, technical skill, and credibility</li> <li>Deliver product and technical briefings / presentations to potential clients</li> <li>Maintain accurate notes and feedback in CRM regarding customer input both wins and losses</li> <li>Proactively engage and communicate with customers and Datadog business/technical teams regarding product feedback and competitive landscape</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;<strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Passionate about educating customers on observability risks that are meaningful to their business, and able to build and execute an evaluation plan with a customer</li> <li>Someone with strong written and oral communication skills. This role requires an ability to understand and articulate both the business benefits (value proposition) and technical advantages of our offering</li> <li>Experienced in programming/scripting with any of the following: Java, Python, Ruby, Go, Node.JS, PHP, and .NET etc.</li> <li>Someone with a minimum of 3+ years in a Sales Engineering or DevOps Engineering role</li> <li>Able to sit up to 4 hours, traveling to and from client sites&nbsp;</li> <li>Able to travel via auto, train or air up to 45% of the time</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.</em></p> <p><strong>Benefits and Growth:</strong>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Best-in-breed onboarding</li> <li>Generous global benefits</li> <li>Intra-departmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking</li> <li>New hire stock equity (RSUs) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)</li> <li>Continuous professional development, product training, and career pathing</li> <li>An inclusive company culture, able to join our Community Guilds and Inclusion Talks</li> </ul> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Hybrid</p><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. jobs.description: <p>The Incident and Escalation Management team (IEM) is part of Datadog's Global Support Engineering (GSE) organization. The purpose of IEM is to continuously improve Datadog's overall customer experience during incidents and critical moments. Datadog Global Support Engineering (GSE) is looking for experts with a background in Incident Management and Escalations handling to join our team to provide fast incident response and stakeholder ownership. In this position, you will play a role in providing communication, technical engagement and incident management for our global customers. You will also implement processes based on the lessons learned from each incident to limit further occurrence. <br><br><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Drive the resolution of incidents and escalations while providing response and/or management to customers</li> <li>Design and implement real-time and proactive monitoring of customer's infrastructures</li> <li>Prioritize, manage, and own customer issues from start to finish</li> <li>Monitor and manage communications (sometimes public, sensitive and large scale) during incidents</li> <li>Collaborate with stakeholders across Datadog and its partners worldwide to enhance customer experience&nbsp;</li> <li>Lead projects to deliver operational improvements as Datadog evolves</li> <li>Create and review documentation as well as incident and escalation training</li> <li>Identify and troubleshoot recurring cloud infrastructure issues</li> <li>Hire and mentor stakeholders and peers in incident and escalation management&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>3+ years demonstrable Incident Manager experience (escalations is a plus)</li> <li>1+ year of experience in Support, Network, Solutions Architecture, or similar IT role</li> <li>Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Science/Technology, Engineering or equivalent experience</li> <li>Familiarity with Cloud computing, experience with Python, JavaScript or shell scripting</li> <li>Ability to correlate behaviors based on known interdependencies&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience driving projects from conception to delivery, demonstrated problem-solving experience, and ability to work in challenging fast-paced environments</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. jobs.description: <p>Datadog is seeking a Manager, Sales Engineering to join our high-growth organization and world-class pre-sales team. You will manage a team of talented Sales Engineers to help qualify and close opportunities and coach the team on their approach to providing technical expertise through sales presentations, product demonstrations, and supporting technical evaluations (POCs). Mentor/coach new hires during on-boarding to ensure proper ramping of skills and capabilities</li> <li>Ensure that your team is enabled to support all required Datadog solutions along with key technical and soft skills</li> <li>Develop a close working relationship with Product Management, Support, and Enablement to ensure continuity between pre and post sales activities</li> <li>Deliver performance reviews along with collaborating on and executing individual development plans</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experienced with 1-3+ years in a Sales Engineering team lead or mentorship role and 3+ years in a Sales Engineering or other equivalent client facing role</li> <li>Coachable, with a strong desire to improve and grow as a professional and a demonstrated ability to navigate through change</li> <li>Proven in your ability to grow and develop a team&nbsp;</li> <li>Experienced in recruiting both individual contributors and front line leaders</li> <li>Knowledgeable in current infrastructure and monitoring solutions and technologies</li> <li>Able to build and execute an evaluation plan with a customer and mentor others on how to do so</li> <li>Someone with strong written and oral communication skills. jobs.title: Manager I, Engineering - Alerting Platform
jobs.description: <p>Engineering Managers in the Alerting Platform are responsible for the core systems that define and schedule monitors, create alerts, and ensure the accuracy and timeliness of the end to end alerting data across the platform.<br><br></p> <p>This engineering manager will lead the team that owns the product experience of defining and managing monitors and the critical first step on customers' journey of responding to issues - a "full stack" team, from the UI to the public API.&nbsp;<br><br></p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships that it builds, the creativity it brings to the table, and the collaboration of being together. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our employees can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.<br><br></em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Define and prioritize goals in long term roadmaps with engineering and product leadership</li> <li>Work with team members to drive technical direction in architecture decision making and ensuring good review processes</li> <li>Manage and grow team of varying experience levels, intern to senior engineers, with a focus on impact and career development</li> <li>Partner extensively across engineering and product teams to understand needs and collaborate on solutions</li> <li>Lead by example in handling incidents and on-call responsibilities, ensure reliability is a priority.<br><br></li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>Built and led teams of 4+ engineers</li> <li>Have owned high-scale customer-facing services that are measured on uptime, performance, and correctness&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience with being on-call, leading incident responses, writing post mortems, and driving remediation efforts</li> <li><em>You have a BS/MS/PhD in a Computer Science, Engineering or related scientific field or equivalent professional experience</em></li> <li>Demonstrated ability to use AI coding tools in day-to-day workflows and validate, critique, and refine AI-generated output</li> <li>You’re motivated to push the boundaries of how AI can improve software engineering best practices and contribute to building AI-enabled products</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.&nbsp;<br></em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Benefits and Growth:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>New hire stock equity (RSUs) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)</li> <li>Continuous professional development, product training, and career pathing</li> <li>Intradepartmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking</li> <li>An inclusive company culture, ability to join our Community Guilds (Datadog employee resource groups)</li> <li>Access to Inclusion Talks, our Internal panel discussions</li> <li>Free, global mental health benefits for employees and dependents age 6+</li> <li>Competitive global benefits</li> </ul> <p><br><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Hybrid</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p>Datadog offers a competitive salary and equity package, and may include variable compensation. Actual compensation is based on factors such as the candidate's skills, qualifications, and experience. jobs.title: Manager I, Engineering - OrgStore Blueprint
jobs.description: <p>The mission of OrgStore is to provide an easy-to-use, fully-managed platform to store and search data. With Postgres as our cornerstone technology, we focus on transactional (OLTP) data use-cases while providing a custom data plane and a flexible administrative layer. Our users are the thousands of engineers representing hundreds of teams producing products at Datadog. As a platform infrastructure team, our goal is to accelerate product teams by helping them rapidly stand up new features and make step change improvements to their application's performance. Data storage is ubiquitous and the service we are providing is critical for Datadog as the business scales and expands its product offerings.</p> <p>As the Engineering Manager for the OrgStore Blueprint Team, you will lead a high-performing team of software engineers, guiding them in building and maintaining these mission-critical systems. Collaboration is key in this role, needing to work closely with other engineering, product, and support teams across Datadog. Your strategic vision will not only guide your team's day-to-day operations but also influence the long-term roadmap of support within Datadog.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships that it builds, the creativity it brings to the table, and the collaboration of being together. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our employees can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Guide and mentor a diverse team of 4-8 software engineers, fostering their career growth while ensuring high team performance.</li> <li>Drive the technical roadmap in collaboration with your team, product managers, and support teams ensuring it aligns with company objectives and directly contributes to improving customer satisfaction.</li> <li>Engage strategically with complex technical problems and work with your team to produce well-defined and actionable plans.</li> <li>Be a core contributor and reviewer of code, lead design decisions, and participate in our&nbsp; well-scoped on-call rotation.</li> <li>Understand the broader business context to align technical decisions with business goals, supporting long-term objectives and driving innovation.</li> <li>Contribute to engineering-wide initiatives as a member of Datadog’s engineering management team.<br><br></li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>A seasoned software engineer with experience building developer tools, running databases, and maintaining infrastructure with a focus on reliability and fault tolerance</li> <li>A proven leader with the ability to manage team dynamics, set goals, provide feedback, and foster a positive and productive work environment</li> <li>A capable project manager who can plan, organize, and oversee multiple software engineering projects</li> <li>An excellent communicator who is comfortable interacting with team members, stakeholders, and upper management alike</li> <li>Demonstrated ability to use AI coding tools in day-to-day workflows and validate, critique, and refine AI-generated output</li> <li>Bonus points: you’re motivated to push the boundaries of how AI can improve software engineering best practices and contribute to building AI-enabled products</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.&nbsp;</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Benefits and Growth:</strong></p> <ul> <li>New hire stock equity (RSUs) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)</li> <li>Continuous professional development, product training, and career pathing</li> <li>Intradepartmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking</li> <li>An inclusive company culture, ability to join our Community Guilds (Datadog employee resource groups)</li> <li>Access to Inclusion Talks, our Internal panel discussions</li> <li>Free, global mental health benefits for employees and dependents age 6+</li> <li>Competitive global benefits</li> </ul> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Hybrid</p><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability.
