engine and core components of our generative AI orchestration platform</li> <li>Collaborate effectively with Product Management, AI researchers and machine learning engineers and designers to build and deliver on the product roadmap</li> <li>Work to develop robust and efficient backend services that orchestrate AI functionalities</li> <li>Identify and address performance bottlenecks and architectural challenges in our systems, particularly within data flow and orchestration</li> <li>Participate actively in code reviews to enforce best practices and patterns</li> <li>Help troubleshoot and resolve complex technical issues in our distributed systems</li> <li>Give and solicit feedback on technical design documents and pull requests</li> <li>Perform tasks related to process such as CI/CD, quality, testing, etc&nbsp;</li> </ul> <h3>Success Measures</h3> <p>Within the first three months, you will have:</p> <ul> <li>Familiarize yourself with the MongoDB database and aggregation language</li> <li>Familiarize yourself with the backend tech stack including Java, Spring Boot, and Kafka</li> <li>Set up software development infrastructure (tech stack, build tools, etc) to enable development using the relevant tech stacks</li> <li>Started collaborating with your peers and contributed to code reviews</li> </ul> <p>Within six months, you will have:</p> <ul> <li>Familiarised yourself with the rest of our the application modernization tool stack</li> <li>Delivered at least one large scale feature that spans the entire tech stack</li> <li>Reviewed and contributed to scope and technical design documents</li> </ul> <p>Within 12 months, you will have:</p> <ul> <li>Become a key contributor to our backend stack, capable of taking on complex features independently</li> <li>Helped recruit and interview new members of the team</li> <li>Collaborated effectively with other teams at MongoDB on cross-functional projects</li> </ul> <h3 class="p1">About MongoDB</h3> <p class="p2">MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the database for the AI era, enabling innovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. MongoDB’s unified database platform—the most widely available, globally distributed database on the market—helps organizations modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our cloud-native platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi-cloud database and is available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.</p> <p class="p2">With offices worldwide and nearly 60,000 customers—including 75% of the Fortune 100 and AI-native startups—relying on MongoDB for their most important applications, we’re powering the next era of software.</p> <p class="p2">Our compass at MongoDB is our <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company">Leadership Commitment,</a> guiding how and why we make decisions, show up for each other, and win. It’s what makes us MongoDB.&nbsp;</p> <p class="p2">To drive the personal growth and business impact of our employees, we’re committed to developing a supportive and enriching culture for everyone. <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company/blog/culture/employee-benefits-that-make-a-difference-at-mongodb">From employee affinity groups, to fertility assistance and a generous parental leave policy</a>, we value our employees’ wellbeing and want to support them along every step of their professional and personal journeys.<a href="https://www.mongodb.com/blog/channel/culture"> Learn more about what it’s like to work at MongoDB</a>, and help us make an impact on the world!</p> <p class="p2">MongoDB is committed to providing any necessary accommodations for individuals with disabilities within our application and interview process. To request an accommodation due to a disability, please inform your recruiter.</p> <p class="p2">MongoDB is an equal opportunities employer.</p> <p>REQ ID: 425547</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p>MongoDB’s base salary range for this role is posted below. Compensation at the time of offer is unique to each candidate and based on a variety of factors such as skill set, experience, qualifications, and work location. Salary is one part of MongoDB’s total compensation and benefits package. Other benefits for eligible employees may include: equity, participation in the employee stock purchase program, flexible paid time off, 20 weeks fully-paid gender-neutral parental leave, fertility and adoption assistance, Registered Retirement Savings Plan (RRSP) with employer match, mental health counseling, backup child and elder care, and health, dental, and vision benefits offerings. Please note, the base salary range listed below and the benefits in this paragraph are only applicable to candidates based in Canada.</p></div><div class="title">MongoDB’s base salary range for this role in Canada is:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$108,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$149,000 CAD</span></div></div></div>

jobs.title: Technical Writer
jobs.description: <p>The Drivers Documentation team is a hands-on and enthusiastic group of engineers who are passionate about teaching developers how to work with data and build applications. We are hiring a “Technical Writer, Drivers” to help us create world-class documentation for MongoDB's developer-focused products -- in particular, MongoDB Drivers and Connectors.</p> <p>Our team works closely with MongoDB's engineering and product organizations to deliver outstanding developer documentation for everyone, regardless of where they are in their software engineering career. Every month, hundreds of thousands of developers turn to our content to help them build microservices, backend and line of business applications, and other projects that are used by millions of people worldwide.</p> <p>We are looking to speak to candidates who are based in Dublin for our hybrid working model.</p> <h3>The team is responsible for the following documentation:&nbsp;</h3> <ul> <li><a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/drivers/">MongoDB Drivers</a>, including <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/drivers/node/current/">Node</a>, <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/drivers/java/sync/current/">Java</a>, <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/drivers/go/current/">Go</a>, <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/drivers/csharp/current/">.NET/C#</a>, and more!&nbsp;</li> <li><a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/spark-connector/current/">MongoDB Spark Connector</a></li> <li>MongoDB <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/core/queryable-encryption/">Queryable Encryption</a> and <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/core/csfle/">Client-Side Field Level Encryption</a></li> </ul> <h3>Candidate Profile:&nbsp;</h3> <p>Candidates must have 3+ years of combined experience in one or more of the following roles: technical writer, developer advocate, and/or&nbsp; software engineer. Beyond industry experience, we are looking for someone who is:</p> <ul> <li>Experienced with developing and shipping modern line-of-business applications OR has extensive experience contributing to complex documentation sites, preferably in a docs-as-code environment</li> <li>Experienced with using great developer documentation and educational materials. You are excited to empower other developers by creating best-in-class documentation</li> <li>Experienced with and enjoys deep investigations of new technical concepts plus teaching others what you’ve learned</li> <li>Eager to keep learning and growing. Passionate about giving and receiving feedback</li> <li>Empathetic with developers of different skill levels and backgrounds to understand what they might find challenging</li> </ul> <h3>Position Expectations:</h3> <ul> <li>Work with technical and non-technical teams to meet the documentation needs of our users</li> <li>Research functionality and features and validate it through testing</li> <li>Develop sample apps &amp; code examples to help users kickstart their projects or refresh themselves on syntax</li> <li>Write documentation for MongoDB <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/drivers/">Drivers</a> and <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/docs/tools-and-connectors/#connectors">Connectors</a>. MongoDB develops many official drivers and connectors that users rely on every day</li> <li>Develop tools that help us create and maintain content</li> </ul> <h3>Success Measures</h3> <ul> <li>Within three months, you’ll be comfortable with our tools and writing style and be able to apply them when authoring new content for MongoDB Drivers and Connectors. You’ll also have completed our company’s required technical onboarding.</li> <li>Within six months, you’ll have gained enough product expertise to propose and deliver new projects while building relationships with the teams that we work with. You’ll also be applying our writing style and coding best practices in both your own work and reviews of your teammates’ work.</li> <li>Within twelve months, you’ll have taken ownership of a subject area and be able to estimate and deliver the related work. By this time, you’ll have performed a full annual cycle of performance and growth conversations with your manager.</li> </ul> <h3>About MongoDB</h3> <p>MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the database for the AI era, enabling innovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. MongoDB’s unified database platform, the most widely available, globally distributed database on the market, helps organizations modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our cloud-native platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi-cloud database and is available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.</p> <p>With offices worldwide and over 60,000 customers, including 75% of the Fortune 100 and AI-native startups, relying on MongoDB for their most important applications, we’re powering the next era of software.</p> <p>Our compass at MongoDB is our <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company">Leadership Commitment,</a> guiding how and why we make decisions, show up for each other, and win. It’s what makes us MongoDB.&nbsp;</p> <p>To drive the personal growth and business impact of our employees, we’re committed to developing a supportive and enriching culture for everyone. <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/company/blog/culture/employee-benefits-that-make-a-difference-at-mongodb">From employee affinity groups, to fertility assistance and a generous parental leave policy</a>, we value our employees’ wellbeing and want to support them along every step of their professional and personal journeys.<a href="https://www.mongodb.com/blog/channel/culture"> Learn more about what it’s like to work at MongoDB</a>, and help us make an impact on the world!</p> <p>MongoDB is committed to providing any necessary accommodations for individuals with disabilities within our application and interview process. To request an accommodation due to a disability, please inform your recruiter.</p> <p>MongoDB is an equal opportunities employer.</p> <p><em>Req ID: 1273396383</em></p>

jobs.title: Operations Manager
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><h2><strong>About Flexport:&nbsp;</strong></h2> <p>At Flexport, we believe global trade can move the human race forward. That’s why it’s our mission to make global commerce so easy there will be more of it. We’re shaping the future of a $10T industry with solutions powered by innovative technology and exceptional people. Today, companies of all sizes—from emerging brands to Fortune 500s—use Flexport technology to move more than $19B of merchandise across 112 countries a year.&nbsp;</p> <p>The recent global supply chain crisis has put Flexport center stage as we continue to play a pivotal role in how goods move around the world. We are proud to have the support of the best investors in the game who believe in our mission, solutions and people. Ready to tackle global challenges that impact business, society, and the environment? Come join us.</p></div><h2><strong>The Opportunity</strong></h2> <p>We are currently seeking highly motivated individuals for a full-time position as an Operations Manager in one of our fulfillment centers. As a key member of the Flexport team, you will take on a leadership role responsible for managing operations and a team on the operating floor. This position offers a unique opportunity to invest in others, cultivate a collaborative leadership style, and contribute to shaping the future of logistics. We are looking for candidates who are eager to take on this challenge, be part of our dynamic and innovative organization, and make a lasting impact on the industry.</p> <h2><strong>You Will</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Lead, coach, manage, and develop a team of 200+ associates and 4+ salaried managers, ensuring their productivity, engagement, and professional growth.</li> <li>Communicate and enforce policies and procedures, serving as the primary source of information for the team, while ensuring compliance, consistency, and taking corrective action when necessary.</li> <li>Create, manage, and support recognition programs to foster a positive work culture and boost employee morale.</li> <li>Implement and maintain safety programs, ensuring compliance with OSHA regulations to provide a safe work environment for all associates.</li> <li>Establish and enforce procedures for building security and product loss prevention, mitigating risks and minimizing inventory shrinkage.</li> <li>Serve as a key member of the Operations team, holding the position of Operations Manager in one of our largest fulfillment centers nationwide.</li> <li>Own and report on performance metrics within the fulfillment center, including on-time receiving and shipping, inventory accuracy, and packing quality.</li> <li>Set and uphold strict Service Level Agreement (SLA) expectations for yourself and your team, delivering clear verbal and written reports on performance and developing action plans as needed.</li> <li>Collaborate with cross-functional teams to troubleshoot and resolve fulfillment-related issues, ensuring smooth operations and customer satisfaction.</li> <li>Act as the escalation point for risk mitigation and issue resolution, providing timely and effective solutions.</li> <li>Identify gaps in inventory accuracy, analyze root causes, and drive strategic initiatives to improve processes and achieve higher accuracy levels.</li> <li>Analyze defect rates, identify trends, and recommend operational improvements internally and across the network to enhance efficiency and reduce errors.</li> <li>Contribute to the development of Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) by documenting best practices and lessons learned, ensuring standardized and optimized processes.</li> <li>Work closely with the Growth and Engineering teams, prioritizing the resolution of systemic problems and driving continuous improvement initiatives.</li> <li>Own CPU and cost metrics.&nbsp;</li> </ul> <h2><strong>You Should Have</strong></h2> <ul> <li>A minimum of 4 years of experience managing a team of 4 or more salaried individuals.</li> <li>Physical capability to lift up to 49 pounds with or without reasonable accommodation.</li> <li>Willingness and ability to frequently engage in physically demanding tasks such as pushing, pulling, squatting, bending, and reaching.</li> <li>Ability to stand/walk for extended periods, typically up to 10-12 hours.</li> <li>Comfort working in an environment with varying noise levels</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>#LI-onsite</p> <p>&nbsp;</p><div class="content-conclusion"><div id="1704749847.557549" class="c-virtual_list__item" data-qa="virtual-list-item" data-item-key="1704749847.557549"> <div class="c-message_kit__background p-message_pane_message__message c-message_kit__message" data-qa="message_container" data-qa-unprocessed="false" data-qa-placeholder="false"> <div class="c-message_kit__hover" data-qa-hover="true"> <div class="c-message_kit__actions c-message_kit__actions--default"> <div class="c-message_kit__gutter"> <div class="c-message_kit__gutter__right" data-qa="message_content"> <div class="c-message_kit__blocks c-message_kit__blocks--rich_text"> <div class="c-message__message_blocks c-message__message_blocks--rich_text" data-qa="message-text"> <div class="p-block_kit_renderer" data-qa="block-kit-renderer"> <div class="p-block_kit_renderer__block_wrapper p-block_kit_renderer__block_wrapper--first"> <div class="p-rich_text_block"> <h4>Commitment to Equal Opportunity</h4> <p>At Flexport, our ability to fulfill our mission of making global commerce easy and accessible relies on having a diverse, dedicated and engaged workforce. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment regardless of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, physical and mental disability, health status, marital and family status, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, military and veteran status, and any other characteristic protected by applicable law.</p> <h4>Global Data Privacy Notice for Job Candidates and Applicants</h4> <p>Depending on your location, the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) may regulate the way we manage the data of job applicants.&nbsp;By submitting your application, you are agreeing to our use and processing of your data as required. Please see our Privacy Notice available at&nbsp;<a href="http://www.flexport.com/privacy" target="_blank" data-saferedirecturl="https://www.google.com/url?q=http://www.flexport.com/privacy&amp;source=gmail&amp;ust=1613840050053000&amp;usg=AFQjCNHD63svlkD-hWHlGuGOM_bSYVrnpw">www.flexport.com/privacy</a>&nbsp;for additional information.</p> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div></div>

jobs.title: Manager I, Engineering - Core Analytics
jobs.description: <p>You will lead a small, hands-on engineering team building the secure, scalable Core Analytics Data Access Platform that accelerates Datadog’s Applied AI and analytics capabilities. The team owns the Data Access Platform — a unified interface that lets AI and analytics teams discover and self-serve production-ready datasets while abstracting underlying systems and embedding required legal and compliance guardrails. In this role you’ll own technical direction, contribute to design and code, and partner closely with Applied AI, Product Analytics, and internal platform teams to provide reliable datasets and APIs for model training and analysis. This role balances day-to-day engineering leadership with long-term platform planning.<br><br></p> <p>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.<br><br></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>Lead a Hands-On Engineering Team:</strong> Manage, mentor, and grow a small team of 2–4 data engineers (mix of senior and junior) across Paris and NYC, fostering technical excellence and career development.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Own Technical Direction and Delivery:</strong> Define architecture, engineering priorities, and the team roadmap for the Data Access Platform, driving implementation of scalable, secure data pipelines and platform services.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Contribute to Design and Code:</strong> Spend substantial time coding, reviewing, and shipping critical platform components to ensure performance, reliability, and operational excellence.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Partner with Internal Stakeholders:</strong> Work closely with Applied AI, Internal Product Analytics, product managers, and platform teams to define data contracts, APIs, SLAs, observability, and curated analytical datasets.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Ensure Data Security, Governance, and Reliability:</strong> Implement access controls, lineage, monitoring, and compliance guardrails to support safe model training and repeatable analytics workflows.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Plan and Scale the Platform:</strong> Evolve storage, processing frameworks, API patterns, and operational practices to enable broader adoption and prepare the platform for increased scope and usage.<br><br><br></li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>Hands-On Engineering Manager</strong> who enjoys managing a small team while remaining an active contributor to design and code.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Experienced in Data Pipelines and At-Scale Data Engineering,</strong> with practical experience building ETL/ELT, streaming and/or batch workflows, and production data servicing layers.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Practical Knowledge of Our Core Stack:</strong> production experience with AWS, Spark, and Iceberg (or equivalent table formats and compute frameworks).<br><br></li> <li><strong>Collaborative Partner to Data Scientists and Analysts:</strong> track record working with applied ML teams, data scientists, and analytics consumers to operationalize data for model training and analysis.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Operationally Strong:</strong> experience defining SLAs, building observability, running incident response, and enforcing data governance in production systems.<br><br></li> <li><strong>Pragmatic Architect and Deliverer:</strong> able to balance short-term delivery with long-term architecture and make tradeoffs that advance product and platform goals.<br><br></li> </ul> <p>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We know not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That’s okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your experience, we encourage you to apply.</p> <p><strong><br><br>Benefits and Growth:</strong></p> <ul> <li>New hire stock equity (RSUs) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)<br><br></li> <li>Continuous professional development, product training, and career pathing<br><br></li> <li>Intradepartmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking<br><br></li> <li>An inclusive company culture, ability to join our Community Guilds (Datadog employee resource groups)<br><br></li> <li>Access to Inclusion Talks, our Internal panel discussions<br><br></li> <li>Free, global mental health benefits for employees and dependents age 6+<br><br></li> <li>Competitive global benefits<br><br></li> </ul> <p>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</p> <p><br>To conform to US export control regulations, candidates should be eligible for any required authorizations from the US government. This job is available in various departments within our company; to conform to US export control regulations, some of these roles may require candidates to be eligible for any required authorizations from the US government.</p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Hybrid</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p>Datadog offers a competitive salary and equity package, and may include variable compensation. Actual compensation is based on factors such as the candidate's skills, qualifications, and experience. In addition, Datadog offers a wide range of best in class, comprehensive and inclusive employee benefits for this role including healthcare, dental, parental planning, and mental health benefits, a 401(k) plan and match, paid time off, fitness reimbursements, and a discounted employee stock purchase plan.</p></div><div class="title">The reasonably estimated yearly salary for this role at Datadog is:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$187,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$240,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. We are on a mission to break down silos and solve complexity in the cloud age by enabling digital transformation, cloud migration, and infrastructure monitoring of our customers’ entire technology stacks. Built by engineers, for engineers, Datadog is used by organizations of all sizes across a wide range of industries. Together, we champion professional development, diversity of thought, innovation, and work excellence to empower continuous growth. Join the pack and become part of a collaborative, pragmatic, and thoughtful people-first community where we solve tough problems, take smart risks, and celebrate one another. Learn more about #DatadogLife on <a href="https://www.instagram.com/datadoghq/?hl=en" target="_blank">Instagram</a>, <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/company/datadog" target="_blank">LinkedIn,</a> and <a href="https://learn.datadoghq.com/" target="_blank">Datadog Learning Center. </a></p> <hr> <p data-renderer-start-pos="215"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Equal Opportunity at Datadog:</strong></p> <p>Datadog is proud to offer <a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf" target="_blank">equal employment opportunity</a> to everyone regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, veteran status, and other characteristics protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. Here are our <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/candidate-legal-notices/" target="_blank">Candidate Legal Notices</a> for your reference.&nbsp;</p> <p>Datadog endeavors to make our Careers Page accessible to all users. If you would like to contact us regarding the accessibility of our website or need assistance completing the application process, please complete <a href="https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeoJeduEZ2NdZF_65uZmWUrCM-zee7yADXX1s6Xr9zAGq8iKA/viewform" target="_blank">this form</a>. This form is for accommodation requests only and cannot be used to inquire about the status of applications.&nbsp;</p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="662"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Privacy and AI Guidelines:</strong></p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="662">Any information you submit to Datadog as part of your application will be processed in accordance with Datadog’s&nbsp;<a class="c-link" href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/applicant-candidate-privacy/" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/applicant-candidate-privacy/" data-sk="tooltip_parent">Applicant and Candidate Privacy Notice</a>. For information on our AI policy, please visit <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/interviewing-at-datadog-ai-guidelines/" target="_blank">Interviewing at Datadog AI Guidelines</a>.</p></div>

jobs.title: Manager II, Engineering - AI Platform Training, Serving and Storage
jobs.description: <p>The AI platform is responsible for all AI infrastructure across Datadog. Our mission is to provide tools and platforms that enable data scientists and engineers to conduct large-scale training and inference with ease. We support products such as <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/datadog-bits-generative-ai/" target="_blank"><u>Bits AI</u></a>, <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/product/llm-observability/" target="_blank"><u>LLMObs</u></a> and all our <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/product/llm-observability/" target="_blank"><u>AI research</u></a>.&nbsp;</p> <p>You’ll join a new and fast growing team that is critical to the future of Datadog. You will support building and scaling the team, help define our technical vision and help shape the roadmap. You will manage other managers and help define the future structure of our organization, participating in the recruitment of the future managers and ICs of the department. You’ll work closely with partner teams in the AI platform organization ensuring a seamless AI development cycle. You’ll also partner with the Applied AI org, product engineering teams, and Datadog infrastructure &amp; tooling teams to build out systems from the ground up.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships that it builds, the creativity it brings to the table, and the collaboration of being together. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our employees can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li> <p>Lead a fast-growing organization around 20 people across 2 teams soon to be 3.&nbsp;</p> </li> <li> <p>You will define the roadmap for your scope and work with ICs and managers to establish the technical direction</p> </li> <li> <p>Collaborate with the engineering team and product manager to define the future of the roadmap</p> </li> <li> <p>Create a strong organizational culture centered around our engineering standards and customer- focused approach</p> </li> <li> <p>Support the whole lifecycle of AI development, including model training, serving, deployment and monitoring</p> </li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Who You Are:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li> <p>You’ve led teams that have built 0 to 1 ML/AI Platforms, with an emphasis on Open Source Tooling (i.e. Ray / AnyScale), versus pure out-of-the-box solutions.&nbsp;</p> </li> <li> <p>An experienced engineer and team player with strong technical skills to influence the technical direction of your teams</p> </li> <li> <p>A people leader with strong interpersonal skills, who has built and led high-performing software engineering teams, including managing managers</p> </li> <li> <p>Interested in working on an early stage project with many challenges to solve and a fast iteration cycle. You bring a strong bias for delivery and make impact through ambiguity.</p> </li> <li> <p>You have a track record of delivering high-quality software on schedule and collaborating closely with product partners.&nbsp;</p> </li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. jobs.description: <p>The AI platform is responsible for all AI infrastructure across Datadog. Our mission is to&nbsp;provide tools and platforms that enable data scientists and engineers to conduct large-scale training and inference with ease. We support products such as <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/datadog-bits-generative-ai/">Bits AI</a>, <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/product/llm-observability/">LLMObs</a> and all our <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/product/llm-observability/">AI research</a>.&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>You’ll join a new and fast growing team that is critical to the future of Datadog. You will support building and scaling the team, help define our technical vision and help shape the roadmap. You will manage other managers and help define the future structure of our organization, participating in the recruitment of the future managers and ICs of the department. You’ll work closely with partner teams in the AI platform organization ensuring a seamless AI development cycle. You’ll also partner with the Applied AI org, product engineering teams, and Datadog infrastructure &amp; tooling teams to build out systems from the ground up.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships that it builds, the creativity it brings to the table, and the collaboration of being together. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our employees can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>Lead a fast-growing organization around 20 people across 2 teams soon to be 3.&nbsp;</li> <li>You will define the roadmap for your scope and work with ICs and managers to establish the technical direction</li> <li>Collaborate with the engineering team and product manager to define the future of the roadmap</li> <li>Create a strong organizational culture centered around our engineering standards and customer- focused approach</li> <li>Support the whole lifecycle of AI development, including model training, serving, deployment and monitoring</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Who You Are:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>You’ve led teams that have built 0 to 1 ML/AI Platforms, with an emphasis on Open Source Tooling (i.e. Ray / AnyScale), versus pure out-of-the-box solutions.&nbsp;</li> <li>An experienced engineer and team player with strong technical skills to influence the technical direction of your teams</li> <li>A people leader with strong interpersonal skills, who has built and led high-performing software engineering teams, including managing managers</li> <li>Interested in working on an early stage project with many challenges to solve and a fast iteration cycle. jobs.title: Manager I, Security Engineering - Vulnerability Management
jobs.description: <p>Here at Datadog, we think about Vulnerability Management a little bit differently. We embrace open source software, and recognize our role in the software supply chain. We also see how attackers are weaponizing vulnerabilities faster than ever before. That’s why we’re looking for a Security Engineering Manager who can help us scale and improve our overall vulnerability management lifecycle, and take an AI-first approach to addressing vulnerability risk.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds, and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Lead, mentor, and grow a team of security engineers responsible for vulnerability management across Datadog’s internal environment, from source code to production.</li> <li>Work with critical partners like SDLC Security, Product Security, and a wide range of engineering teams to “shift left” and reduce upstream vulnerabilities entering our ecosystem.</li> <li>Reduce engineering toil related to vulnerability remediation through a “PRs, not tickets” approach.</li> <li>Improve the efficiency of our overall vulnerability management lifecycle through thoughtful use of automation and AI.</li> <li>Develop metrics and reporting to provide leadership with an accurate view of overall vulnerability risk.</li> <li>Provide evidence and subject matter expertise for vulnerability management processes and controls for multiple compliance frameworks (SOC2, HIPAA, PCI, FedRAMP, ISO)</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>You have experience with vulnerability management or similar domains like cloud security posture management, SDLC management, etc.</li> <li>You have excellent leadership, mentorship, and coaching skills, with the ability to foster a high-performing security team for a large software ecosystem</li> <li>You have strong communication skills, with the ability to convey technical information clearly and effectively to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.</li> <li>You have a proven track record of managing cross-functional collaboration between security, engineering, and product teams.</li> <li>You are experienced in one or more programming languages (Golang, Python, Java, etc.)</li> <li>You are a U.S. person and able to work on ITAR-controlled projects</li> <li>You have a BS/MS/PhD in a Computer Science, Engineering or related scientific field or equivalent experience</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. jobs.description: <p><strong>The Opportunity:</strong></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>We are seeking a Real Estate Associate Project Manager to help the Real Estate team optimize coworking, on-demand and direct office services globally.&nbsp; This is a critical role within our global Real Estate team.&nbsp; The Real Estate Associate Project Manager will be responsible for all coworking initiatives in Europe and Asia.&nbsp; This includes small real estate project management and real estate administration in a dynamic environment.&nbsp; The role reports to the Vice President, Global Real Estate.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Responsibilities:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Manage Coworking access&nbsp; for EMEA, APAC as well as backup for AMER and LATAM coworking support to ensure our employees utilizing coworking spaces are fully supported&nbsp;</li> <li>Coordinate EMEA and APAC Coworking floorplans and services with Design &amp; Construction and Office Operations on small projects</li> <li>Complete Weekly Capacity Updates of all Coworking locations where applicable&nbsp;</li> <li>Coordinate with Accounts Payable to update Credit Card information for Coworking and assist with Invoicing Issues</li> <li>Maintain our Datadog Coworking Members summary, tracking all activity including adding/deleting members, expanding/reducing capacity, adding/deleting locations, pay on demand members, rents, etc.</li> <li>Own Global Real Estate Coworking vendor onboarding and Purchase Order creation</li> <li>Monitor and report usage of all Coworking locations in EMEA and APAC</li> <li>Analyze Pay on Demand monthly reporting and make recommendations from that data to maximize efficiency, manage costs, etc.</li> <li>Visit locations as required for site evaluations, location inspections, meet with teams, and to address issues</li> <li>Ensure coworking offices are serviced per the terms of the lease Update all Real Estate Vendor, Landlord Contact Lists and Quarterly Review Materials</li> <li>Drive innovation and continuous improvement by bringing ideas forward to help differentiate the Datadog Real Estate team through efficiency and scalability to operate as, and deliver “best in class” results</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Your experience and qualifications:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Minimum of a Degree in Business, Finance, Real Estate or Law&nbsp;</li> <li>Minimum of 2 jobs.description: <p>Datadog’s Revenue Operations team is seeking an innovative and passionate Salesforce expert, who will impact a rapidly growing environment and its connected third-party systems.&nbsp; This individual will proactively engage and support our internal customer needs and ensure we are delivering high quality solutions. They manage the CRM with the team to maintain existing features/products for the business, and partner with stakeholders to design and implement new global initiatives, and drive global quality improvement processes.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds, and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>Partner with GTM Ecosystem teams: Sales, Customer Success, Channels &amp; Alliances, Marketing, Sales Enablement, Product, Engineering, and Finance to analyze and optimize the processes related to Lead-to-Opportunity</li> <li>Optimize daily processes by developing and implementing automation solutions&nbsp;</li> <li>Become subject matter expert for Sales Go-To-Market and post sales processes in Salesforce through day to day support of internal and internal Users</li> <li>Troubleshoot, reproduce, and resolve problems reported by users in a timely manner</li> <li>Perform user acceptance testing (UAT), deployments, and tech stack audits with the team to ensure business requirements are met and upheld</li> <li>Prepare data from various internal and external sources in order to import updated data into existing databases, using data load, etc.</li> <li>Create and maintain documentation on processes, policies, and application configuration</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>BA/BS in Business, Engineering, or other quantitative field</li> <li>2-3 years experience in Salesforce, including any combination of configuration and development, flow automation, user management, and report generation</li> <li>Strong knowledge of Sales business processes in the opportunity-to-close cycle</li> <li>Outstanding ability to partner with business stakeholders to understand business process challenges and provide guidance on technical solutions</li> <li>Knowledge of CI/CD methodology</li> <li>Salesforce Administrator Certification</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. jobs.title: Software Engineer - Early Career
jobs.description: <p>We’re looking for early-career engineers to help us collect, aggregate, visualize, and analyze high-scale metrics, logs, and application data. Software engineering at Datadog includes a variety of exciting opportunities. Interested in distributed systems or Kubernetes across tens of thousands of nodes? Looking to build out products like Real User Monitoring or Cloud Security Management or perhaps join one of our internal teams helping engineering teams ship apps quickly? Want to see your work actually impact and improve the product in a meaningful way? We’ve got you covered. Whether you’re writing a new integration, building data pipelines, or working with the open-source community, our early-career engineers are key contributors to our product.</p> <p>Datadog offers the unique opportunity to work on large-scale, real-time systems by engineers for engineers. Our product is multi-cloud and collects more than a hundred trillion events per day for 28,000+ customers. Early-career engineers work towards the same objectives as their senior teammates to bring lasting impact to our products.</p> <p>We place value in our office culture - the relationships that it builds, the creativity it brings to the table, and the collaboration of being together. Our hybrid workplace ensures our employees can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</p> <p>Students can sign up anytime for a free <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/datadog-github-student-developer-pack/" target="_blank">Datadog Pro account</a>!</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Solve a scaling bottleneck in a critical service</li> <li>Deploy a new feature to production and progressively roll it out with feature flags</li> <li>Investigate and fix a production issue from a service your team owns</li> <li>Create a way to optimize a service to handle more traffic at scale</li> <li>In partnership with your team, plan the most important projects to work on next</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>You are in the process of receiving/have a degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering or related scientific field, or equivalent experience</li> <li>You are targeting a 2026 full-time start date</li> <li>You are comfortable coding in one or more languages&nbsp;</li> <li>You value code simplicity and performance</li> <li>You want to work in a fast-paced environment that values its employees and customers</li> <li>You are a self-starter who enjoys partnering with other engineers and solving difficult problems</li> <li>You’re excited about leveraging AI tools to enhance how you code, solve problems, and build – or eager to learn how</li> <li>You’re looking for or have some exposure using AI coding tools in day-to-day workflows and validate, critique, and refine AI-generated output</li> <li>Bonus: you’re motivated to push the boundaries of how AI can improve software engineering best practices and contribute to building AI-enabled products</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. and advocate for your customers’ needs with Datadog’s Product and Engineering teams.</li> <li>Create executive-level summaries and insights that tie platform usage to business outcomes.</li> The Technical Solutions team enables Datadog’s worldwide growth by educating potential clients and ensuring that existing customers are happy and successful. As a Technical Account Manager 2 (TAM 2), you’ll serve as a trusted advisor to our strategic customers, accelerating their adoption of the Datadog platform and enabling long-term success. TAM 2s bring deep technical expertise, refined customer skills, and consultative insight into how monitoring, observability, and DevOps practices translate to business value.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture—the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Act as a technical advisor to 3 primary enterprise accounts, ensuring successful product adoption and effective usage of the Datadog platform.</li> <li>Lead enablement and adoption sessions across core product areas tailored to your customer’s architecture and business needs.</li> <li>Analyze customers’ IT Operations environments and workflows to recommend configuration, product usage, and performance improvements.</li> <li>Deliver technical business reviews, health checks, and account maturity assessments, contributing to customer QBRs with impactful recommendations.</li> <li>Escalate product issues appropriately and advocate for your customers’ needs with Datadog’s Product and Engineering teams.</li> <li>Create executive-level summaries and insights that tie platform usage to business outcomes.</li> <li>Participate in internal TAM strategy sessions and contribute to team learning through feature presentations, case studies, or best-practice sharing</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>You have 2+ years of experience in a technical customer-facing role (TAM, Solutions Architect, SRE, DevOps Engineer, or similar) within the cloud or observability space.</li> <li>You’re confident with at least two public cloud platforms (e.g. AWS, Azure, GCP) and have hands-on experience with infrastructure or application monitoring.</li> <li>You’re familiar with scripting or automation tools (e.g. Python, Bash, Terraform, Chef/Puppet).</li> <li>You have working knowledge of CI/CD pipelines and containerization tools (e.g. Jenkins, GitLab, Docker, Kubernetes).</li> <li>You excel at communicating technical concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, and can adapt your style for individual contributors or exec audiences.</li> <li>You’re able to work with minimal guidance on customer enablement, issue triage, and product adoption strategies.</li> <li>You are comfortable traveling up to 25–30% of the time as needed (via auto, air, or train).</li> <li>Previous experience using Datadog or other cloud monitoring tools is a strong plus</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.</em></p> <p><strong>Benefits and Growth:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Best-in-breed onboarding program</li> <li>Generous global benefits package</li> <li>Intra-departmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking</li> <li>New hire stock equity (RSUs) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)</li> <li>Continuous professional development, product training, and career pathing</li> <li>An inclusive company culture, including Community Guilds and Inclusion Talks</li> </ul> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Hybrid</p><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. We are on a mission to break down silos and solve complexity in the cloud age by enabling digital transformation, cloud migration, and infrastructure monitoring of our customers’ entire technology stacks. Built by engineers, for engineers, Datadog is used by organizations of all sizes across a wide range of industries. Together, we champion professional development, diversity of thought, innovation, and work excellence to empower continuous growth. Join the pack and become part of a collaborative, pragmatic, and thoughtful people-first community where we solve tough problems, take smart risks, and celebrate one another. Learn more about #DatadogLife on <a href="https://www.instagram.com/datadoghq/?hl=en" target="_blank">Instagram</a>, <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/company/datadog" target="_blank">LinkedIn,</a> and <a href="https://learn.datadoghq.com/" target="_blank">Datadog Learning Center. </a></p> <hr> <p data-renderer-start-pos="215"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Equal Opportunity at Datadog:</strong></p> <p>Datadog is proud to offer <a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf" target="_blank">equal employment opportunity</a> to everyone regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, veteran status, and other characteristics protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. Here are our <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/candidate-legal-notices/" target="_blank">Candidate Legal Notices</a> for your reference.&nbsp;</p> <p>Datadog endeavors to make our Careers Page accessible to all users. If you would like to contact us regarding the accessibility of our website or need assistance completing the application process, please complete <a href="https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeoJeduEZ2NdZF_65uZmWUrCM-zee7yADXX1s6Xr9zAGq8iKA/viewform" target="_blank">this form</a>. This form is for accommodation requests only and cannot be used to inquire about the status of applications.&nbsp;</p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="662"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Privacy and AI Guidelines:</strong></p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="662">Any information you submit to Datadog as part of your application will be processed in accordance with Datadog’s&nbsp;<a class="c-link" href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/applicant-candidate-privacy/" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/applicant-candidate-privacy/" data-sk="tooltip_parent">Applicant and Candidate Privacy Notice</a>. For information on our AI policy, please visit <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/interviewing-at-datadog-ai-guidelines/" target="_blank">Interviewing at Datadog AI Guidelines</a>.</p></div>
